## Entry 161
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:28:48.124Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:28:48.124Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_efZvuXBNZT96wz9wuouHlRoo",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 0d1ec9\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3928\nOutput:\n### 5.6 ThinkingIndicator\n\nShown while awaiting agent response.\n\n- Three animated dots (CSS pulse) + label \"Analyzing sensor data…\" in IBM Plex Sans 13px, colour `#4A5568`.\n- Wrap in `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Agent is analyzing sensor data\"` so screen readers announce it without being disruptive.\n- Disappears as soon as the first streaming token arrives.\n\n### 5.7 InputBar\n\n- Sticky bottom, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-top: 1px solid #D1DBE8`.\n- `<textarea>` auto-resizes (1–4 rows). Background `#F7F9FC`, border `1px solid #C9D6E3`, text `#0F1F2E`. Placeholder text `#6B7B8D` (contrast 4.6:1 — AA).\n- Visible `:focus` ring: `outline: 2px solid #005F8E`, `outline-offset: 2px` — never `outline: none`.\n- Placeholder text: `\"Ask about flood risk, AQI, traffic, power…\"`.\n- **Send button:** background `#005F8E`, icon + text label \"Send\" (visually hidden text for screen readers via `aria-label=\"Send message\"`). Disabled state: `#C9D6E3` background, `#6B7B8D` text, `aria-disabled=\"true\"`. `Enter` submits, `Shift+Enter` newline.\n- **Abort:** while streaming, button becomes \"Stop\" (square icon, `background: #DC2626`, `aria-label=\"Stop response\"`). Never rely on colour alone — icon + label both change.\n\n### 5.8 SuggestedPrompts\n\nShown only in the empty state (no messages yet). These reflect the UrbanClimate AI's core capabilities.\n\nFour chips in a 2×2 grid:\n- \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded?\"\n- \"How does current humidity compare to pre-monsoon averages of the last decade?\"\n- \"Which logistics routes through Bengaluru are highest risk today?\"\n- \"Generate a risk scorecard for Sarjapur Road.\"\n\n> **Note:** Only the last prompt reliably triggers a RiskCard — it explicitly requests a structured scorecard. The others are natural language queries that return streamed text. Chips are for user convenience, not a trigger mechanism.\n\nClicking a chip populates and submits the textarea.\n\n---\n\n## 6. API Contract\n\n### POST `/api/chat`\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response (streaming — `text/event-stream` SSE):**\n```\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \"Based on the 2022 flood post-mortem\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \" and current drainage capacity...\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"risk_card\", \"payload\": { ...structured risk JSON... }}\ndata: {\"type\": \"done\"}\n```\n\n**Error response:**\n```json\n{ \"error\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"...\" }\n```\n\nThe frontend handles five SSE event types:\n- `token` — append content to the current assistant bubble's text.\n- `risk_card` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `riskCard` and render a RiskCard below the streamed text. The frontend must never emit or synthesize this event itself. A single response can contain both `token` events and one `risk_card` event. The `risk_card` event may arrive before, during, or after the token stream — the frontend must handle all orderings gracefully.\n- `artifact` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `artifact` and render an `ArtifactRenderer` below any RiskCard. Payload schema: `{ type: \"html\" | \"jsx\", title: string, source: string, description?: string }`. The frontend must sanitise/sandbox the content before rendering (see §5.5). A response may contain at most one `artifact` event; if multiple arrive, render only the last one and log a warning.\n- `done` — mark message complete, re-enable input.\n- `error` — show inline error banner in the message bubble.\n\n**GET `/api/health`** — used by StatusPill to poll agent connectivity (poll every 30s).\n\n---\n\n## 7. Risk Card JSON Schema\n\nThe agent emits this as a `risk_card` SSE event payload when it determines a structured neighborhood scorecard is the appropriate response. The frontend renders it as a RiskCard component. All top-level metric fields are optional — the agent only populates fields it has sufficient data to compute.\n\n```typescript\ninterface RiskCardPayload {\n  neighborhood: string;           // e.g. \"SARJAPUR ROAD\", \"BELLANDUR\", \"MANYATA TECH PARK\"\n  generated_at: string;           // ISO 8601 — timestamp of agent analysis\n  overall_risk_score: number;     // 0–10 composite score\n\n  // Cross-reference: DB rainfall forecast + drainage capacity docs + lake telemetry\n  flood_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Cross-reference: wind-speed telemetry + tree-density maps + historical outage logs\n  power_outage_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: rainfall threshold / traffic bottleneck historical correlation\n  traffic_delay_index?: {\n    congestion_score: number;     // 0–10\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: AQI sensor data + Urban Heat Island research docs\n  health_advisory?: {\n    aqi: number;                  // raw AQI value\n    aqi_category: string;         // e.g. \"GOOD\", \"MODERATE\", \"UNHEALTHY FOR SENSITIVE GROUPS\", \"UNHEALTHY\", \"HAZARDOUS\"\n  };\n\n  // Agent's final recommendations based on full cross-analysis\n  emergency_readiness?: {\n    recommendation: string;       // e.g. \"Evacuate low-lying zones; barricade underpasses\"\n    actions?: string[];           // Specific action items, e.g. [\"Barricade Silk Board underpass\", \"Pre-position pumps at Bellandur inlet\"]\n  };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. State Management\n\nNo external state library needed. Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n\n```typescript\n// ChatPage state\nconst [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\nconst [input, setInput] = useState('');\nconst [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);\nconst [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'error'>('connecting');\nconst sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\nconst abortController = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n```\n\n```typescript\ninterface ArtifactPayload {\n  type: 'html' | 'jsx';\n  title: string;\n  source: string;              // raw HTML or JSX string from the agent\n  description?: string;        // plain-text alt for screen readers / error fallback\n}\n\ninterface Message {\n  id: string;\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant';\n  content: string;             // accumulated text\n  riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;  // optional structured block\n  artifact?: ArtifactPayload;  // optional chart / visualisation artifact\n  timestamp: Date;\n  isStreaming?: boolean;\n}\n```\n\nArtifact parse state (parsing/transpiling is async) is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n\n---\n\n## 9. File Structure\n\n```\nclient/src/\n├── App.tsx                        # Single route → ChatPage\n├── main.tsx                       # Providers (ThemeProvider, etc.)\n├── lib/\n│   └── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold) + SSE helper\n├── hooks/\n│   ├── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send, stream, abort, session\n│   └── useArtifactParser.ts       # Parses/transpiles ArtifactPayload → renderable output\n├── components/\n│   ├── layout/\n│   │   └── Header.tsx\n│   ├── chat/\n│   │   ├── MessageList.tsx\n│   │   ├── MessageBubble.tsx\n│   │   ├── RiskCard.tsx\n│   │   ├── ArtifactRenderer.tsx   # Sandboxed iframe (HTML) or evaluated JSX renderer\n│   │   ├── ThinkingIndicator.tsx\n│   │   └── SuggestedPrompts.tsx\n│   └── input/\n│       └── InputBar.tsx\n├── pages/\n│   └── ChatPage.tsx\n├── types/\n│   └── chat.ts                    # Message, RiskCardPayload, ArtifactPayload interfaces\n├── lib/\n│   └── artifactSandbox.ts         # DOMPurify sanitiser + @babel/standalone JSX transpiler wrapper\n└── theme/\n    └── metrosense.ts              # MUI theme overrides\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Styling\n\n### 10.1 Colour Tokens\n\nAll values verified at WCAG 2.1 AA (text ≥ 4.5:1, UI components ≥ 3:1).\n\n| Token | Value | Usage |\n|-------|-------|-------|\n| `--bg-page` | `#F7F9FC` | Page / app background |\n| `--bg-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Cards, bubbles, panels |\n| `--bg-sunken` | `#EEF2F7` | User bubble, input field |\n| `--border` | `#D1DBE8` | All dividers and borders |\n| `--border-strong` | `#C9D6E3` | Input fields, card outlines |\n| `--text-primary` | `#0F1F2E` | All body text (15.9:1 on `--bg-page`) |\n| `--text-secondary` | `#4A5568` | Labels, timestamps (7.1:1 on `--bg-page`) |\n| `--text-placeholder` | `#6B7B8D` | Input placeholder (4.6:1 — AA) |\n| `--accent` | `#005F8E` | Primary action, links, AI bubble border (6.6:1 on white) |\n| `--accent-hover` | `#004A70` | Hover/active state |\n| `--accent-text` | `#FFFFFF` | Text on `--accent` buttons (6.9:1) |\n| `--sev-critical-bg` | `#FFF0F2` | CRITICAL badge background |\n| `--sev-critical-text` | `#9B1D2A` | CRITICAL badge text (7.3:1) |\n| `--sev-critical-bar` | `#C0213A` | CRITICAL progress bar |\n| `--sev-high-bg` | `#FFF4ED` | HIGH badge background |\n| `--sev-high-text` | `#7C3005` | HIGH badge text (8.5:1) |\n| `--sev-high-bar` | `#C2520C` | HIGH progress bar |\n| `--sev-moderate-bg` | `#FFFBEB` | MODERATE badge background |\n| `--sev-moderate-text` | `#6B4C00` | MODERATE badge text (7.6:1) |\n| `--sev-moderate-bar` | `#B07D0A` | MODERATE progress bar |\n| `--sev-low-bg` | `#F0FDF4` | LOW badge background |\n| `--sev-low-text` | `#14532D` | LOW badge text (8.7:1) |\n| `--sev-low-bar` | `#16863E` | LOW progress bar |\n\n### 10.2 MUI Theme Override\n\n```typescript\n// theme/metrosense.ts\n{\n  palette: {\n    mode: 'light',\n    background: { default: '#F7F9FC', paper: '#FFFFFF' },\n    primary: { main: '#005F8E', contrastText: '#FFFFFF' },\n    error:   { main: '#C0213A' },\n    warning: { main: '#B07D0A' },\n    success: { main: '#16863E' },\n    text: { primary: '#0F1F2E', secondary: '#4A5568', disabled: '#6B7B8D' }\n  },\n  typography: {\n    fontFamily: \"'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif\",\n    fontSize: 14,\n    // Mono variant: sx={{ fontFamily: 'IBM Plex Mono, monospace' }}\n  },\n  components: {\n    MuiButtonBase: {\n      defaultProps: { disableRipple: false },\n      styleOverrides: {\n        root: {\n          '&:focus-visible': {\n            outline: '2px solid #005F8E',\n            outlineOffset: '2px',\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 10.3 Accessibility Requirements\n\n- **Focus rings:** Every interactive element must show a visible `2px solid #005F8E` focus ring on keyboard focus. Never suppress `outline`.\n- **Colour independence:** Every status, severity, and state must also communicate via text label or icon — not colour alone.\n- **Touch targets:** All buttons and chips minimum `44 × 44px` tap target (WCAG 2.5.5).\n- **Motion:** Wrap all non-essential animations in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` — disable or reduce them.\n- **Semantic HTML:** Use `<main>`, `<header>`, `<section aria-label>` landmark roles. Message list: `role=\"log\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Conversation\"`. Streaming indicator: `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\"`.\n- **Minimum font size:** 13px for any visible text. Secondary/timestamp labels may be 11px only if colour contrast ≥ 7:1.\n- **Link underlines:** Inline links in message text must be underlined (not colour-only).\n- **Custom scrollbar:** Thin, `#C9D6E3` track, `#005F8E` thumb. Ensure scrollbar thumb contrast ≥ 3:1 against track.\n\n---\n\n## 11. Environment Variables\n\nAdd to the scaffold's existing env var pattern:\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agent:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Only used if agent runs as a separate process; omit if in-process. |\n| `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | *(proxied via Vite → :8010)* | Frontend API base (already handled by Vite proxy in scaffold) |\n\n---\n\n## 12. Backend Route to Add\n\nAdd one route to `server/app/api/routes/`:\n\n```\nserver/app/api/routes/chat.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.stream_chat()`.\n- Stream the SSE response back to the client.\n- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state.\n\nAdd one service:\n\n```\nserver/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- `async def stream_chat(session_id, message) → AsyncIterator[str]`\n- **If Agent Server runs in-process:** import and call it directly — no HTTP overhead.\n- **If Agent Server runs as a separate process on localhost:** call `http://localhost:8020/chat` (internal only, never crosses the internet).\n- Handles timeout (30s), connection errors → emits `{\"type\": \"error\", ...}` on failure.\n\nAdd to `docker-compose.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nagent:\n  build: ./agent          # your Agent Server directory\n  ports:\n    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal only; not exposed if in-process\n  depends_on:\n    - db\n  environment:\n    - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Out of Scope (Not in this spec)\n\n- Authentication / user accounts.\n- Chat history persistence (no DB writes for chat messages).\n- Map rendering (separate concern — existing MetroSense dashboard).\n- Mobile-specific layout (responsive is sufficient — no native app).\n- Multi-agent routing or tool call visibility in the UI.\n- **Keyword-based or client-side RiskCard triggering.** The frontend must never inspect message content to decide whether to show a RiskCard. That decision belongs entirely to the agent.\n- **Artifact execution beyond the approved library whitelist.** The JSX artifact parser exposes only `React`, `ReactDOM`, `recharts`, `victory`, and `d3`. Any artifact requiring other libraries (e.g. Mapbox, Leaflet, Plotly) is out of scope for the inline renderer — the agent should not produce such artifacts. If it does, `ArtifactRenderer` must show the error state, not attempt execution.\n- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers only parsing and rendering on the frontend. How the agent decides to produce an artifact (and what chart library it uses) is defined in the agent spec, not here.\n- **Multi-artifact messages.** A single agent response renders at most one artifact. If the agent sends multiple `artifact` events in one response, only the last is rendered.\n\n---\n\n## 14. Implementation Order\n\n1. `theme/metrosense.ts` — get the design tokens right first.\n2. `Header.tsx` + `StatusPill` — static shell.\n3. `types/chat.ts` — lock the interfaces including `ArtifactPayload`.\n4. `useChat.ts` hook — SSE streaming logic (mock the backend with a local stub first); handle `artifact` event alongside `risk_card` and `token`.\n5. `InputBar.tsx` + `SuggestedPrompts.tsx`.\n6. `MessageBubble.tsx` + `ThinkingIndicator.tsx`.\n7. `RiskCard.tsx`.\n8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper + `@babel/standalone` JSX transpiler; write unit tests for the sanitiser and the scope-limited evaluator before wiring into the UI.\n9. `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts` — async hook that takes an `ArtifactPayload` and returns `{ status: 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error', element: ReactNode | null, error?: string }`.\n10. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n11. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n12. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n13. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration."
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_efZvuXBNZT96wz9wuouHlRoo",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 0d1ec9\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 3928\nOutput:\n### 5.6 ThinkingIndicator\n\nShown while awaiting agent response.\n\n- Three animated dots (CSS pulse) + label \"Analyzing sensor data…\" in IBM Plex Sans 13px, colour `#4A5568`.\n- Wrap in `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Agent is analyzing sensor data\"` so screen readers announce it without being disruptive.\n- Disappears as soon as the first streaming token arrives.\n\n### 5.7 InputBar\n\n- Sticky bottom, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-top: 1px solid #D1DBE8`.\n- `<textarea>` auto-resizes (1–4 rows). Background `#F7F9FC`, border `1px solid #C9D6E3`, text `#0F1F2E`. Placeholder text `#6B7B8D` (contrast 4.6:1 — AA).\n- Visible `:focus` ring: `outline: 2px solid #005F8E`, `outline-offset: 2px` — never `outline: none`.\n- Placeholder text: `\"Ask about flood risk, AQI, traffic, power…\"`.\n- **Send button:** background `#005F8E`, icon + text label \"Send\" (visually hidden text for screen readers via `aria-label=\"Send message\"`). Disabled state: `#C9D6E3` background, `#6B7B8D` text, `aria-disabled=\"true\"`. `Enter` submits, `Shift+Enter` newline.\n- **Abort:** while streaming, button becomes \"Stop\" (square icon, `background: #DC2626`, `aria-label=\"Stop response\"`). Never rely on colour alone — icon + label both change.\n\n### 5.8 SuggestedPrompts\n\nShown only in the empty state (no messages yet). These reflect the UrbanClimate AI's core capabilities.\n\nFour chips in a 2×2 grid:\n- \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded?\"\n- \"How does current humidity compare to pre-monsoon averages of the last decade?\"\n- \"Which logistics routes through Bengaluru are highest risk today?\"\n- \"Generate a risk scorecard for Sarjapur Road.\"\n\n> **Note:** Only the last prompt reliably triggers a RiskCard — it explicitly requests a structured scorecard. The others are natural language queries that return streamed text. Chips are for user convenience, not a trigger mechanism.\n\nClicking a chip populates and submits the textarea.\n\n---\n\n## 6. API Contract\n\n### POST `/api/chat`\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response (streaming — `text/event-stream` SSE):**\n```\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \"Based on the 2022 flood post-mortem\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \" and current drainage capacity...\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"risk_card\", \"payload\": { ...structured risk JSON... }}\ndata: {\"type\": \"done\"}\n```\n\n**Error response:**\n```json\n{ \"error\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"...\" }\n```\n\nThe frontend handles five SSE event types:\n- `token` — append content to the current assistant bubble's text.\n- `risk_card` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `riskCard` and render a RiskCard below the streamed text. The frontend must never emit or synthesize this event itself. A single response can contain both `token` events and one `risk_card` event. The `risk_card` event may arrive before, during, or after the token stream — the frontend must handle all orderings gracefully.\n- `artifact` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `artifact` and render an `ArtifactRenderer` below any RiskCard. Payload schema: `{ type: \"html\" | \"jsx\", title: string, source: string, description?: string }`. The frontend must sanitise/sandbox the content before rendering (see §5.5). A response may contain at most one `artifact` event; if multiple arrive, render only the last one and log a warning.\n- `done` — mark message complete, re-enable input.\n- `error` — show inline error banner in the message bubble.\n\n**GET `/api/health`** — used by StatusPill to poll agent connectivity (poll every 30s).\n\n---\n\n## 7. Risk Card JSON Schema\n\nThe agent emits this as a `risk_card` SSE event payload when it determines a structured neighborhood scorecard is the appropriate response. The frontend renders it as a RiskCard component. All top-level metric fields are optional — the agent only populates fields it has sufficient data to compute.\n\n```typescript\ninterface RiskCardPayload {\n  neighborhood: string;           // e.g. \"SARJAPUR ROAD\", \"BELLANDUR\", \"MANYATA TECH PARK\"\n  generated_at: string;           // ISO 8601 — timestamp of agent analysis\n  overall_risk_score: number;     // 0–10 composite score\n\n  // Cross-reference: DB rainfall forecast + drainage capacity docs + lake telemetry\n  flood_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Cross-reference: wind-speed telemetry + tree-density maps + historical outage logs\n  power_outage_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: rainfall threshold / traffic bottleneck historical correlation\n  traffic_delay_index?: {\n    congestion_score: number;     // 0–10\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: AQI sensor data + Urban Heat Island research docs\n  health_advisory?: {\n    aqi: number;                  // raw AQI value\n    aqi_category: string;         // e.g. \"GOOD\", \"MODERATE\", \"UNHEALTHY FOR SENSITIVE GROUPS\", \"UNHEALTHY\", \"HAZARDOUS\"\n  };\n\n  // Agent's final recommendations based on full cross-analysis\n  emergency_readiness?: {\n    recommendation: string;       // e.g. \"Evacuate low-lying zones; barricade underpasses\"\n    actions?: string[];           // Specific action items, e.g. [\"Barricade Silk Board underpass\", \"Pre-position pumps at Bellandur inlet\"]\n  };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. State Management\n\nNo external state library needed. Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n\n```typescript\n// ChatPage state\nconst [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\nconst [input, setInput] = useState('');\nconst [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);\nconst [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'error'>('connecting');\nconst sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\nconst abortController = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n```\n\n```typescript\ninterface ArtifactPayload {\n  type: 'html' | 'jsx';\n  title: string;\n  source: string;              // raw HTML or JSX string from the agent\n  description?: string;        // plain-text alt for screen readers / error fallback\n}\n\ninterface Message {\n  id: string;\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant';\n  content: string;             // accumulated text\n  riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;  // optional structured block\n  artifact?: ArtifactPayload;  // optional chart / visualisation artifact\n  timestamp: Date;\n  isStreaming?: boolean;\n}\n```\n\nArtifact parse state (parsing/transpiling is async) is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n\n---\n\n## 9. File Structure\n\n```\nclient/src/\n├── App.tsx                        # Single route → ChatPage\n├── main.tsx                       # Providers (ThemeProvider, etc.)\n├── lib/\n│   └── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold) + SSE helper\n├── hooks/\n│   ├── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send, stream, abort, session\n│   └── useArtifactParser.ts       # Parses/transpiles ArtifactPayload → renderable output\n├── components/\n│   ├── layout/\n│   │   └── Header.tsx\n│   ├── chat/\n│   │   ├── MessageList.tsx\n│   │   ├── MessageBubble.tsx\n│   │   ├── RiskCard.tsx\n│   │   ├── ArtifactRenderer.tsx   # Sandboxed iframe (HTML) or evaluated JSX renderer\n│   │   ├── ThinkingIndicator.tsx\n│   │   └── SuggestedPrompts.tsx\n│   └── input/\n│       └── InputBar.tsx\n├── pages/\n│   └── ChatPage.tsx\n├── types/\n│   └── chat.ts                    # Message, RiskCardPayload, ArtifactPayload interfaces\n├── lib/\n│   └── artifactSandbox.ts         # DOMPurify sanitiser + @babel/standalone JSX transpiler wrapper\n└── theme/\n    └── metrosense.ts              # MUI theme overrides\n```\n\n---\n\n## 10. Styling\n\n### 10.1 Colour Tokens\n\nAll values verified at WCAG 2.1 AA (text ≥ 4.5:1, UI components ≥ 3:1).\n\n| Token | Value | Usage |\n|-------|-------|-------|\n| `--bg-page` | `#F7F9FC` | Page / app background |\n| `--bg-surface` | `#FFFFFF` | Cards, bubbles, panels |\n| `--bg-sunken` | `#EEF2F7` | User bubble, input field |\n| `--border` | `#D1DBE8` | All dividers and borders |\n| `--border-strong` | `#C9D6E3` | Input fields, card outlines |\n| `--text-primary` | `#0F1F2E` | All body text (15.9:1 on `--bg-page`) |\n| `--text-secondary` | `#4A5568` | Labels, timestamps (7.1:1 on `--bg-page`) |\n| `--text-placeholder` | `#6B7B8D` | Input placeholder (4.6:1 — AA) |\n| `--accent` | `#005F8E` | Primary action, links, AI bubble border (6.6:1 on white) |\n| `--accent-hover` | `#004A70` | Hover/active state |\n| `--accent-text` | `#FFFFFF` | Text on `--accent` buttons (6.9:1) |\n| `--sev-critical-bg` | `#FFF0F2` | CRITICAL badge background |\n| `--sev-critical-text` | `#9B1D2A` | CRITICAL badge text (7.3:1) |\n| `--sev-critical-bar` | `#C0213A` | CRITICAL progress bar |\n| `--sev-high-bg` | `#FFF4ED` | HIGH badge background |\n| `--sev-high-text` | `#7C3005` | HIGH badge text (8.5:1) |\n| `--sev-high-bar` | `#C2520C` | HIGH progress bar |\n| `--sev-moderate-bg` | `#FFFBEB` | MODERATE badge background |\n| `--sev-moderate-text` | `#6B4C00` | MODERATE badge text (7.6:1) |\n| `--sev-moderate-bar` | `#B07D0A` | MODERATE progress bar |\n| `--sev-low-bg` | `#F0FDF4` | LOW badge background |\n| `--sev-low-text` | `#14532D` | LOW badge text (8.7:1) |\n| `--sev-low-bar` | `#16863E` | LOW progress bar |\n\n### 10.2 MUI Theme Override\n\n```typescript\n// theme/metrosense.ts\n{\n  palette: {\n    mode: 'light',\n    background: { default: '#F7F9FC', paper: '#FFFFFF' },\n    primary: { main: '#005F8E', contrastText: '#FFFFFF' },\n    error:   { main: '#C0213A' },\n    warning: { main: '#B07D0A' },\n    success: { main: '#16863E' },\n    text: { primary: '#0F1F2E', secondary: '#4A5568', disabled: '#6B7B8D' }\n  },\n  typography: {\n    fontFamily: \"'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif\",\n    fontSize: 14,\n    // Mono variant: sx={{ fontFamily: 'IBM Plex Mono, monospace' }}\n  },\n  components: {\n    MuiButtonBase: {\n      defaultProps: { disableRipple: false },\n      styleOverrides: {\n        root: {\n          '&:focus-visible': {\n            outline: '2px solid #005F8E',\n            outlineOffset: '2px',\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 10.3 Accessibility Requirements\n\n- **Focus rings:** Every interactive element must show a visible `2px solid #005F8E` focus ring on keyboard focus. Never suppress `outline`.\n- **Colour independence:** Every status, severity, and state must also communicate via text label or icon — not colour alone.\n- **Touch targets:** All buttons and chips minimum `44 × 44px` tap target (WCAG 2.5.5).\n- **Motion:** Wrap all non-essential animations in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` — disable or reduce them.\n- **Semantic HTML:** Use `<main>`, `<header>`, `<section aria-label>` landmark roles. Message list: `role=\"log\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Conversation\"`. Streaming indicator: `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\"`.\n- **Minimum font size:** 13px for any visible text. Secondary/timestamp labels may be 11px only if colour contrast ≥ 7:1.\n- **Link underlines:** Inline links in message text must be underlined (not colour-only).\n- **Custom scrollbar:** Thin, `#C9D6E3` track, `#005F8E` thumb. Ensure scrollbar thumb contrast ≥ 3:1 against track.\n\n---\n\n## 11. Environment Variables\n\nAdd to the scaffold's existing env var pattern:\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agent:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Only used if agent runs as a separate process; omit if in-process. |\n| `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | *(proxied via Vite → :8010)* | Frontend API base (already handled by Vite proxy in scaffold) |\n\n---\n\n## 12. Backend Route to Add\n\nAdd one route to `server/app/api/routes/`:\n\n```\nserver/app/api/routes/chat.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.stream_chat()`.\n- Stream the SSE response back to the client.\n- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state.\n\nAdd one service:\n\n```\nserver/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- `async def stream_chat(session_id, message) → AsyncIterator[str]`\n- **If Agent Server runs in-process:** import and call it directly — no HTTP overhead.\n- **If Agent Server runs as a separate process on localhost:** call `http://localhost:8020/chat` (internal only, never crosses the internet).\n- Handles timeout (30s), connection errors → emits `{\"type\": \"error\", ...}` on failure.\n\nAdd to `docker-compose.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nagent:\n  build: ./agent          # your Agent Server directory\n  ports:\n    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal only; not exposed if in-process\n  depends_on:\n    - db\n  environment:\n    - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 13. Out of Scope (Not in this spec)\n\n- Authentication / user accounts.\n- Chat history persistence (no DB writes for chat messages).\n- Map rendering (separate concern — existing MetroSense dashboard).\n- Mobile-specific layout (responsive is sufficient — no native app).\n- Multi-agent routing or tool call visibility in the UI.\n- **Keyword-based or client-side RiskCard triggering.** The frontend must never inspect message content to decide whether to show a RiskCard. That decision belongs entirely to the agent.\n- **Artifact execution beyond the approved library whitelist.** The JSX artifact parser exposes only `React`, `ReactDOM`, `recharts`, `victory`, and `d3`. Any artifact requiring other libraries (e.g. Mapbox, Leaflet, Plotly) is out of scope for the inline renderer — the agent should not produce such artifacts. If it does, `ArtifactRenderer` must show the error state, not attempt execution.\n- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers only parsing and rendering on the frontend. How the agent decides to produce an artifact (and what chart library it uses) is defined in the agent spec, not here.\n- **Multi-artifact messages.** A single agent response renders at most one artifact. If the agent sends multiple `artifact` events in one response, only the last is rendered.\n\n---\n\n## 14. Implementation Order\n\n1. `theme/metrosense.ts` — get the design tokens right first.\n2. `Header.tsx` + `StatusPill` — static shell.\n3. `types/chat.ts` — lock the interfaces including `ArtifactPayload`.\n4. `useChat.ts` hook — SSE streaming logic (mock the backend with a local stub first); handle `artifact` event alongside `risk_card` and `token`.\n5. `InputBar.tsx` + `SuggestedPrompts.tsx`.\n6. `MessageBubble.tsx` + `ThinkingIndicator.tsx`.\n7. `RiskCard.tsx`.\n8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper + `@babel/standalone` JSX transpiler; write unit tests for the sanitiser and the scope-limited evaluator before wiring into the UI.\n9. `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts` — async hook that takes an `ArtifactPayload` and returns `{ status: 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error', element: ReactNode | null, error?: string }`.\n10. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n11. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n12. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n13. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration."
  }
}
```

## Entry 168
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:29:02.583Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:29:02.583Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_g7VSCb8YoS1UBXvaVD0A67jH",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 7963f4\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 4117\nOutput:\n     1\t# FRONTEND_SPEC.md — MetroSense Chat Interface\n     2\t\n     3\t## 1. Overview\n     4\t\n     5\tA minimal, single-page chat interface for the **UrbanClimate AI** system, serving city planners, BBMP disaster teams, and logistics companies in evaluating weather-related risks and infrastructure readiness for Bengaluru.\n     6\t\n     7\tUsers interact via natural language. The agent queries a local PostgreSQL database (real-time sensor data: temperature, humidity, wind speed, rainfall, AQI, lake water levels, power outage logs, traffic heatmaps) and retrieves context from local documentation (IMD weather bulletins, BBMP drainage master plans, flood post-mortem reports, urban forest maps, climate resilience whitepapers). The UI is a **plain chat interface** — it shows streamed text responses for the vast majority of queries.\n     8\t\n     9\tThe chat interface has **two structured output types** beyond plain streamed text — both are agent-decided and never client-inferred:\n    10\t\n    11\t1. **RiskCard** — rendered when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event carrying a neighborhood risk scorecard as structured JSON (flood probability, power outage risk, traffic delay index, health advisory, emergency readiness).\n    12\t2. **Artifact** — rendered when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event carrying a self-contained HTML or JSX string representing a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation. The frontend parses and sandboxes this markup and renders it inline in the conversation. The agent produces these when a visual answer (e.g. a rainfall trend chart, an AQI heatmap, a congestion timeline) is more useful than prose.\n    13\t\n    14\tThe frontend never infers, guesses, or keyword-triggers either output type — the SSE event type is the sole rendering signal.\n    15\t\n    16\t**Design language:** Clean, accessible light theme. Background `#F7F9FC`, white card surfaces, slate-navy `#0F1F2E` text, steel-blue `#005F8E` primary accent. IBM Plex Sans for body copy, IBM Plex Mono for data/labels. All colours meet WCAG 2.1 AA (≥ 4.5:1 for text, ≥ 3:1 for UI components). No colour-only status signals — every state is communicated with colour + icon + text label.\n    17\t\n    18\t---\n    19\t\n    20\t## 2. Architecture\n    21\t\n    22\t```\n    23\tUser (Browser)\n    24\t    │\n    25\t    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n    26\t    ▼\n    27\tFastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n    28\t    │\n    29\t    │  in-process call (Python import, not HTTP)\n    30\t    ▼\n    31\tAgent Server  (agent/)              ← same repo, same Docker Compose\n    32\t    │\n    33\t    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, Redis, sensor APIs\n    34\t    ▼\n    35\tAgent Response  (streamed text + optional structured JSON block + optional artifact HTML/JSX)\n    36\t```\n    37\t\n    38\tThe Agent Server is a **first-party process you own** — running in the same Docker Compose stack. The FastAPI backend calls it **in-process** (direct Python import) or over localhost if you choose to run it as a separate uvicorn process on a different port (e.g. `:8020`). Either way, the frontend never calls it directly — all chat traffic goes through `POST /api/chat` on the FastAPI backend.\n    39\t\n    40\t---\n    41\t\n    42\t## 3. Pages / Routes\n    43\t\n    44\t| Route | Component | Description |\n    45\t|-------|-----------|-------------|\n    46\t| `/` | `ChatPage` | The only page. Full-height chat interface. |\n    47\t\n    48\tNo routing library needed beyond React Router with a single route, or just a single-page render.\n    49\t\n    50\t---\n    51\t\n    52\t## 4. Component Tree\n    53\t\n    54\t```\n    55\tApp\n    56\t└── ChatPage\n    57\t    ├── Header\n    58\t    │   ├── Logo (\"METROSENSE\" + sub-label)\n    59\t    │   ├── StatusPill (agent connection status)\n    60\t    │   └── SessionId (mono, dimmed)\n    61\t    ├── MessageList  (scrollable, flex-col)\n    62\t    │   ├── MessageBubble (role: user | assistant)\n    63\t    │   │   ├── TextContent       (markdown-rendered)\n    64\t    │   │   ├── RiskCard?         (optional — agent risk_card SSE event)\n    65\t    │   │   └── ArtifactRenderer? (optional — agent artifact SSE event)\n    66\t    │   └── ThinkingIndicator  (streaming / loading state)\n    67\t    └── InputBar\n    68\t        ├── TextArea  (auto-resize, max 4 rows)\n    69\t        ├── SendButton\n    70\t        └── SuggestedPrompts  (shown only when MessageList is empty)\n    71\t```\n    72\t\n    73\t---\n    74\t\n    75\t## 5. Component Specifications\n    76\t\n    77\t### 5.1 Header\n    78\t\n    79\t- Sticky top, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`, `box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.\n    80\t- **Logo:** \"METROSENSE\" in IBM Plex Sans 600 20px, colour `#0F1F2E`. Sub-label: \"BENGALURU CLIMATE INTELLIGENCE\" in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`.\n    81\t- **StatusPill:** Icon (✓ / spinner / ⚠) + colour tint + text label — never colour alone. States:\n    82\t  - Online: `#F0FDF4` background, `#14532D` text, `#16A34A` left border, label \"Agent Online\"\n    83\t  - Connecting: `#FFF9EB` background, `#6B4C00` text, `#CA8A04` left border, label \"Connecting…\"\n    84\t  - Degraded: `#FFF0F2` background, `#9B1D2A` text, `#DC2626` left border, label \"Degraded\"\n    85\t- **SessionId:** `SESSION · <id[:8]>` in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`. Tap to copy — expose `aria-label=\"Copy session ID\"` and a visible focus ring on the button.\n    86\t\n    87\t### 5.2 MessageList\n    88\t\n    89\t- `overflow-y: auto`, flex column, padding `24px 0`, gap between bubbles `16px`.\n    90\t- Auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages.\n    91\t- Maintains scroll position if user has scrolled up (don't yank them down).\n    92\t\n    93\t### 5.3 MessageBubble\n    94\t\n    95\t**User bubble:**\n    96\t- Right-aligned.\n    97\t- Background: `#EEF2F7`, border: `1px solid #C9D6E3`, border-radius `12px 12px 2px 12px`.\n    98\t- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E` (contrast 14.1:1 on `#EEF2F7` — AAA).\n    99\t- Timestamp: IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`, below bubble.\n   100\t\n   101\t**Assistant bubble:**\n   102\t- Left-aligned, slightly wider (max-width 80%).\n   103\t- Background: `#FFFFFF`, left border: `3px solid #005F8E`.\n   104\t- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E`.\n   105\t- Renders Markdown: bold, inline code, bullet lists, code blocks styled with mono font and `#F7F9FC` background with `#D1DBE8` border.\n   106\t- **RiskCard rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains a `risk_card` event. The frontend does not inspect message text or keywords — the presence of `riskCard` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition.\n   107\t- **Artifact rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains an `artifact` event. The presence of `artifact` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition. Rendered via `ArtifactRenderer` (see §5.5) below the RiskCard if both are present. A single message may contain streamed text, a RiskCard, an Artifact, or any combination.\n   108\t\n   109\t### 5.4 RiskCard\n   110\t\n   111\tRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event.** This is the structured output for a neighborhood risk scorecard. It is never shown for plain conversational responses — not for flood questions, not for AQI questions, not for any keyword match. The agent decides when a scorecard is warranted.\n   112\t\n   113\tThe card surfaces four risk dimensions derived from the agent's cross-analysis of sensor data and documentation:\n   114\t\n   115\t```\n   116\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   117\t│  SARJAPUR ROAD  ·  RISK SCORE: 9.1 ◼    │  ← neighborhood + overall score badge\n   118\t├─────────────────────────────────────────┤\n   119\t│  FLOOD         ████████░░  94%  CRITICAL │  ← flood_risk (DB rainfall + drainage docs)\n   120\t│  POWER OUTAGE  ██████░░░░  72%  HIGH     │  ← power_outage_risk (wind telemetry + outage logs)\n   121\t│  TRAFFIC       ████████░░  8.4  HIGH     │  ← traffic_delay_index (congestion heatmaps)\n   122\t│  AQI           █████░░░░░  187  UNHEALTHY│  ← health_advisory (AQI sensors + UHI research)\n   123\t└─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n   124\t```\n   125\t\n   126\t- Each row: label (mono) + progress bar (tinted per severity) + value + severity badge.\n   127\t- **Accessible severity system** — every severity uses a dark text on a light tinted background badge. Never colour alone; the text label is always present:\n   128\t\n   129\t  | Severity | Badge bg | Badge text | Bar fill | Min contrast |\n   130\t  |----------|----------|------------|----------|--------------|\n   131\t  | CRITICAL | `#FFF0F2` | `#9B1D2A` | `#C0213A` | 7.3:1 |\n   132\t  | HIGH     | `#FFF4ED` | `#7C3005` | `#C2520C` | 8.5:1 |\n   133\t  | MODERATE | `#FFFBEB` | `#6B4C00` | `#B07D0A` | 7.6:1 |\n   134\t  | LOW      | `#F0FDF4` | `#14532D` | `#16863E` | 8.7:1 |\n   135\t\n   136\t- Card background: `#FFFFFF`, border: `1px solid #D1DBE8`, border-radius `8px`, `box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.\n   137\t- Header row background: `#F7F9FC`.\n   138\t- If `emergency_readiness.recommendation` is present, render it as a footer row below the metric rows, background `#F0F6FF`, text `#0F1F2E`.\n   139\t- If `emergency_readiness.actions` is present, render as a compact bulleted list below the recommendation.\n   140\t\n   141\t**What each field represents:**\n   142\t- `flood_risk` — agent's cross-reference of predicted rainfall intensity against drainage capacity documents (BBMP master plan, 2022 flood post-mortems) and lake water level telemetry.\n   143\t- `power_outage_risk` — probability derived from wind-speed telemetry merged with tree-density maps and historical outage incident logs.\n   144\t- `traffic_delay_index` — estimated delay factor from rainfall-threshold/traffic-bottleneck correlation in historical congestion heatmaps.\n   145\t- `health_advisory` — AQI sensor reading synthesized with Urban Heat Island research docs; `aqi_category` surfaces the advisory level for sensitive populations.\n   146\t- `emergency_readiness` — agent's final recommendation and specific action items (e.g., underpass barricading, evacuation routes).\n   147\t\n   148\t### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n   149\t\n   150\tRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event.** The agent produces artifacts when a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation communicates the answer better than prose — for example, a 7-day rainfall trend chart, a neighbourhood AQI heatmap, or a congestion timeline by hour.\n   151\t\n   152\t#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n   153\t\n   154\tThe `artifact` SSE payload carries a self-contained string of either HTML or JSX. The agent sets a `type` field to indicate which:\n   155\t\n   156\t```typescript\n   157\tinterface ArtifactPayload {\n   158\t  type: \"html\" | \"jsx\";\n   159\t  title: string;                 // e.g. \"Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)\"\n   160\t  source: string;                // the raw HTML or JSX string\n   161\t  description?: string;          // optional plain-text alt description for screen readers\n   162\t}\n   163\t```\n   164\t\n   165\tExamples of artifacts the agent might produce:\n   166\t- A Recharts `<LineChart>` of hourly rainfall over the past 48 hours\n   167\t- An HTML `<table>` of neighbourhood AQI readings ranked by severity\n   168\t- A `<BarChart>` of historical flood incidents per zone\n   169\t- An SVG choropleth of traffic congestion across Bengaluru\n   170\t\n   171\t#### 5.5.2 Parser (`useArtifactParser` hook)\n   172\t\n   173\tThe hook lives at `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts`. It receives an `ArtifactPayload` and returns a resolved, safe render target.\n   174\t\n   175\t**For `type: \"html\"`:**\n   176\t1. Receive the raw HTML string from the agent.\n   177\t2. Sanitise with DOMPurify (strip `<script>`, event handlers, external resource URLs) — never trust agent-produced markup directly.\n   178\t3. Render inside a sandboxed `<iframe srcdoc={sanitisedHtml} sandbox=\"allow-scripts allow-same-origin\" />`. The iframe must have `title={artifact.title}` for screen readers.\n   179\t4. The iframe auto-sizes to fit its content via a `ResizeObserver` posted message from inside the iframe.\n   180\t\n   181\t**For `type: \"jsx\"`:**\n   182\t1. Receive the JSX string. This is expected to be a self-contained Recharts / Victory / plain React component — no imports, no external fetches.\n   183\t2. Use `@babel/standalone` to transpile the JSX string to plain JS at runtime (already bundled — no network call).\n   184\t3. Evaluate the transpiled JS in a controlled scope that provides only: `React`, `ReactDOM`, and a pre-approved chart library whitelist (`recharts`, `victory`, `d3` — nothing else). Evaluation uses `new Function(...)` with an explicit scope object — never `eval()` directly.\n   185\t4. Render the resulting React element into a dedicated `<div>` inside a `React.Suspense` boundary with an error boundary wrapping it.\n   186\t5. On any parse or render error, fall back to the `description` field as plain text, or a generic \"Chart could not be rendered\" message if no description is present.\n   187\t\n   188\t**Security rules (non-negotiable):**\n   189\t- HTML artifacts: DOMPurify sanitisation before any insertion into the DOM.\n   190\t- JSX artifacts: only the approved library scope is exposed to the evaluated code — no `window`, no `document`, no `fetch`.\n   191\t- Neither artifact type may load external URLs (network requests, images from remote origins, fonts).\n   192\t- Content Security Policy header on the app must include `sandbox` restrictions covering artifact iframes.\n   193\t\n   194\t#### 5.5.3 ArtifactRenderer component\n   195\t\n   196\t```\n   197\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   198\t│  📊  Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)    │  ← title bar\n   199\t│                                          [⤢ expand] │\n   200\t├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n   201\t│                                                 │\n   202\t│         [rendered chart / graph here]           │\n   203\t│                                                 │\n   204\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n   205\t```\n   206\t\n   207\t- **Title bar:** `#F7F9FC` background, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`. Icon prefix (📊 for charts, 📋 for tables, 🗺 for maps). Title in IBM Plex Sans 13px `#0F1F2E`. Expand button `[⤢]` opens the artifact full-screen in a Modal (`aria-modal=\"true\"`).\n   208\t- **Content area:** White background, `min-height: 200px`, `max-height: 480px`, overflow hidden (scrollable if content exceeds). For iframes, set `width: 100%`, `height: auto` via ResizeObserver.\n   209\t- **Error state:** If parsing or rendering fails, show a bordered `#FFF0F2` panel with icon ⚠, message \"Chart could not be rendered\", and the `description` text if available.\n   210\t- **Loading state:** Skeleton shimmer while the parser/transpiler is working.\n   211\t- **Accessibility:** `role=\"figure\"`, `aria-label={artifact.title}`. If `description` is present, include a visually hidden `<figcaption>` for screen readers. Expand button: `aria-label=\"Expand chart: {title}\"`.\n   212\t\n   213\t#### 5.5.4 Expand / full-screen Modal\n   214\t\n   215\t- Triggered by the `[⤢]` button or keyboard `Enter`/`Space` on it.\n   216\t- MUI `<Dialog fullWidth maxWidth=\"lg\">`, `aria-modal=\"true\"`, `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title.\n   217\t- Focus is trapped inside the modal while open. `Escape` closes it and returns focus to the expand button.\n   218\t- Modal renders the same artifact at full width (re-uses the same parsed/transpiled output — no second parse).\n   219\t\n   220\t### 5.6 ThinkingIndicator\n"
  }
}
```

Overview\n     4\t\n     5\tA minimal, single-page chat interface for the **UrbanClimate AI** system, serving city planners, BBMP disaster teams, and logistics companies in evaluating weather-related risks and infrastructure readiness for Bengaluru.\n     6\t\n     7\tUsers interact via natural language. The agent queries a local PostgreSQL database (real-time sensor data: temperature, humidity, wind speed, rainfall, AQI, lake water levels, power outage logs, traffic heatmaps) and retrieves context from local documentation (IMD weather bulletins, BBMP drainage master plans, flood post-mortem reports, urban forest maps, climate resilience whitepapers). The UI is a **plain chat interface** — it shows streamed text responses for the vast majority of queries.\n     8\t\n     9\tThe chat interface has **two structured output types** beyond plain streamed text — both are agent-decided and never client-inferred:\n    10\t\n    11\t1. **RiskCard** — rendered when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event carrying a neighborhood risk scorecard as structured JSON (flood probability, power outage risk, traffic delay index, health advisory, emergency readiness).\n    12\t2. **Artifact** — rendered when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event carrying a self-contained HTML or JSX string representing a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation. The frontend parses and sandboxes this markup and renders it inline in the conversation. The agent produces these when a visual answer (e.g. a rainfall trend chart, an AQI heatmap, a congestion timeline) is more useful than prose.\n    13\t\n    14\tThe frontend never infers, guesses, or keyword-triggers either output type — the SSE event type is the sole rendering signal.\n    15\t\n    16\t**Design language:** Clean, accessible light theme. Background `#F7F9FC`, white card surfaces, slate-navy `#0F1F2E` text, steel-blue `#005F8E` primary accent. IBM Plex Sans for body copy, IBM Plex Mono for data/labels. All colours meet WCAG 2.1 AA (≥ 4.5:1 for text, ≥ 3:1 for UI components). No colour-only status signals — every state is communicated with colour + icon + text label.\n    17\t\n    18\t---\n    19\t\n    20\t## 2. Architecture\n    21\t\n    22\t```\n    23\tUser (Browser)\n    24\t    │\n    25\t    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n    26\t    ▼\n    27\tFastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n    28\t    │\n    29\t    │  in-process call (Python import, not HTTP)\n    30\t    ▼\n    31\tAgent Server  (agent/)              ← same repo, same Docker Compose\n    32\t    │\n    33\t    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, Redis, sensor APIs\n    34\t    ▼\n    35\tAgent Response  (streamed text + optional structured JSON block + optional artifact HTML/JSX)\n    36\t```\n    37\t\n    38\tThe Agent Server is a **first-party process you own** — running in the same Docker Compose stack. The FastAPI backend calls it **in-process** (direct Python import) or over localhost if you choose to run it as a separate uvicorn process on a different port (e.g. `:8020`). Either way, the frontend never calls it directly — all chat traffic goes through `POST /api/chat` on the FastAPI backend.\n    39\t\n    40\t---\n    41\t\n    42\t## 3. Pages / Routes\n    43\t\n    44\t| Route | Component | Description |\n    45\t|-------|-----------|-------------|\n    46\t| `/` | `ChatPage` | The only page. (optional — agent artifact SSE event)\n    66\t    │   └── ThinkingIndicator  (streaming / loading state)\n    67\t    └── InputBar\n    68\t        ├── TextArea  (auto-resize, max 4 rows)\n    69\t        ├── SendButton\n    70\t        └── SuggestedPrompts  (shown only when MessageList is empty)\n    71\t```\n    72\t\n    73\t---\n    74\t\n    75\t## 5. Component Specifications\n    76\t\n    77\t### 5.1 Header\n    78\t\n    79\t- Sticky top, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`, `box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.\n    80\t- **Logo:** \"METROSENSE\" in IBM Plex Sans 600 20px, colour `#0F1F2E`. Sub-label: \"BENGALURU CLIMATE INTELLIGENCE\" in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`.\n    81\t- **StatusPill:** Icon (✓ / spinner / ⚠) + colour tint + text label — never colour alone. States:\n    82\t  - Online: `#F0FDF4` background, `#14532D` text, `#16A34A` left border, label \"Agent Online\"\n    83\t  - Connecting: `#FFF9EB` background, `#6B4C00` text, `#CA8A04` left border, label \"Connecting…\"\n    84\t  - Degraded: `#FFF0F2` background, `#9B1D2A` text, `#DC2626` left border, label \"Degraded\"\n    85\t- **SessionId:** `SESSION · <id[:8]>` in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`. Tap to copy — expose `aria-label=\"Copy session ID\"` and a visible focus ring on the button.\n    86\t\n    87\t### 5.2 MessageList\n    88\t\n    89\t- `overflow-y: auto`, flex column, padding `24px 0`, gap between bubbles `16px`.\n    90\t- Auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages.\n    91\t- Maintains scroll position if user has scrolled up (don't yank them down).\n    92\t\n    93\t### 5.3 MessageBubble\n    94\t\n    95\t**User bubble:**\n    96\t- Right-aligned.\n    97\t- Background: `#EEF2F7`, border: `1px solid #C9D6E3`, border-radius `12px 12px 2px 12px`.\n    98\t- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E` (contrast 14.1:1 on `#EEF2F7` — AAA).\n    99\t- Timestamp: IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`, below bubble.\n   100\t\n   101\t**Assistant bubble:**\n   102\t- Left-aligned, slightly wider (max-width 80%).\n   103\t- Background: `#FFFFFF`, left border: `3px solid #005F8E`.\n   104\t- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E`.\n   105\t- Renders Markdown: bold, inline code, bullet lists, code blocks styled with mono font and `#F7F9FC` background with `#D1DBE8` border.\n   106\t- **RiskCard rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains a `risk_card` event. The frontend does not inspect message text or keywords — the presence of `riskCard` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition.\n   107\t- **Artifact rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains an `artifact` event. The presence of `artifact` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition. Rendered via `ArtifactRenderer` (see §5.5) below the RiskCard if both are present. A single message may contain streamed text, a RiskCard, an Artifact, or any combination.\n   108\t\n   109\t### 5.4 RiskCard\n   110\t\n   111\tRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event.** This is the structured output for a neighborhood risk scorecard. It is never shown for plain conversational responses — not for flood questions, not for AQI questions, not for any keyword match. The agent decides when a scorecard is warranted.\n   112\t\n   113\tThe card surfaces four risk dimensions derived from the agent's cross-analysis of sensor data and documentation:\n   114\t\n   115\t```\n   116\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   117\t│  SARJAPUR ROAD  ·  RISK SCORE: 9.1 ◼    │  ← neighborhood + overall score badge\n   118\t├─────────────────────────────────────────┤\n   119\t│  FLOOD         ████████░░  94%  CRITICAL │  ← flood_risk (DB rainfall + drainage docs)\n   120\t│  POWER OUTAGE  ██████░░░░  72%  HIGH     │  ← power_outage_risk (wind telemetry + outage logs)\n   121\t│  TRAFFIC       ████████░░  8.4  HIGH     │  ← traffic_delay_index (congestion heatmaps)\n   122\t│  AQI           █████░░░░░  187  UNHEALTHY│  ← health_advisory (AQI sensors + UHI research)\n   123\t└─────────────────────────────────────────┘\n   124\t```\n   125\t\n   126\t- Each row: label (mono) + progress bar (tinted per severity) + value + severity badge.\n   127\t- **Accessible severity system** — every severity uses a dark text on a light tinted background badge. Never colour alone; the text label is always present:\n   128\t\n   129\t  | Severity | Badge bg | Badge text | Bar fill | Min contrast |\n   130\t  |----------|----------|------------|----------|--------------|\n   131\t  | CRITICAL | `#FFF0F2` | `#9B1D2A` | `#C0213A` | 7.3:1 |\n   132\t  | HIGH     | `#FFF4ED` | `#7C3005` | `#C2520C` | 8.5:1 |\n   133\t  | MODERATE | `#FFFBEB` | `#6B4C00` | `#B07D0A` | 7.6:1 |\n   134\t  | LOW      | `#F0FDF4` | `#14532D` | `#16863E` | 8.7:1 |\n   135\t\n   136\t- Card background: `#FFFFFF`, border: `1px solid #D1DBE8`, border-radius `8px`, `box-shadow: 0 2px 8px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.\n   137\t- Header row background: `#F7F9FC`.\n   138\t- If `emergency_readiness.recommendation` is present, render it as a footer row below the metric rows, background `#F0F6FF`, text `#0F1F2E`.\n   139\t- If `emergency_readiness.actions` is present, render as a compact bulleted list below the recommendation.\n   140\t\n   141\t**What each field represents:**\n   142\t- `flood_risk` — agent's cross-reference of predicted rainfall intensity against drainage capacity documents (BBMP master plan, 2022 flood post-mortems) and lake water level telemetry.\n   143\t- `power_outage_risk` — probability derived from wind-speed telemetry merged with tree-density maps and historical outage incident logs.\n   144\t- `traffic_delay_index` — estimated delay factor from rainfall-threshold/traffic-bottleneck correlation in historical congestion heatmaps.\n   145\t- `health_advisory` — AQI sensor reading synthesized with Urban Heat Island research docs; `aqi_category` surfaces the advisory level for sensitive populations.\n   146\t- `emergency_readiness` — agent's final recommendation and specific action items (e.g., underpass barricading, evacuation routes).\n   147\t\n   148\t### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n   149\t\n   150\tRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event.** The agent produces artifacts when a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation communicates the answer better than prose — for example, a 7-day rainfall trend chart, a neighbourhood AQI heatmap, or a congestion timeline by hour.\n   151\t\n   152\t#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n   153\t\n   154\tThe `artifact` SSE payload carries a self-contained string of either HTML or JSX. The agent sets a `type` field to indicate which:\n   155\t\n   156\t```typescript\n   157\tinterface ArtifactPayload {\n   158\t  type: \"html\" | \"jsx\";\n   159\t  title: string;                 // e.g. \"Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)\"\n   160\t  source: string;                // the raw HTML or JSX string\n   161\t  description?: string;          // optional plain-text alt description for screen readers\n   162\t}\n   163\t```\n   164\t\n   165\tExamples of artifacts the agent might produce:\n   166\t- A Recharts `<LineChart>` of hourly rainfall over the past 48 hours\n   167\t- An HTML `<table>` of neighbourhood AQI readings ranked by severity\n   168\t- A `<BarChart>` of historical flood incidents per zone\n   169\t- An SVG choropleth of traffic congestion across Bengaluru\n   170\t\n   171\t#### 5.5.2 Parser (`useArtifactParser` hook)\n   172\t\n   173\tThe hook lives at `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts`. It receives an `ArtifactPayload` and returns a resolved, safe render target.\n   174\t\n   175\t**For `type: \"html\"`:**\n   176\t1. Receive the raw HTML string from the agent.\n   177\t2. Sanitise with DOMPurify (strip `<script>`, event handlers, external resource URLs) — never trust agent-produced markup directly.\n   178\t3. Render inside a sandboxed `<iframe srcdoc={sanitisedHtml} sandbox=\"allow-scripts allow-same-origin\" />`. The iframe must have `title={artifact.title}` for screen readers.\n   179\t4. The iframe auto-sizes to fit its content via a `ResizeObserver` posted message from inside the iframe.\n   180\t\n   181\t**For `type: \"jsx\"`:**\n   182\t1. Receive the JSX string. This is expected to be a self-contained Recharts / Victory / plain React component — no imports, no external fetches.\n   183\t2. Use `@babel/standalone` to transpile the JSX string to plain JS at runtime (already bundled — no network call).\n   184\t3. Evaluate the transpiled JS in a controlled scope that provides only: `React`, `ReactDOM`, and a pre-approved chart library whitelist (`recharts`, `victory`, `d3` — nothing else). Evaluation uses `new Function(...)` with an explicit scope object — never `eval()` directly.\n   185\t4. Render the resulting React element into a dedicated `<div>` inside a `React.Suspense` boundary with an error boundary wrapping it.\n   186\t5. On any parse or render error, fall back to the `description` field as plain text, or a generic \"Chart could not be rendered\" message if no description is present.\n   187\t\n   188\t**Security rules (non-negotiable):**\n   189\t- HTML artifacts: DOMPurify sanitisation before any insertion into the DOM.\n   190\t- JSX artifacts: only the approved library scope is exposed to the evaluated code — no `window`, no `document`, no `fetch`.\n   191\t- Neither artifact type may load external URLs (network requests, images from remote origins, fonts).\n   192\t- Content Security Policy header on the app must include `sandbox` restrictions covering artifact iframes.\n   193\t\n   194\t#### 5.5.3 ArtifactRenderer component\n   195\t\n   196\t```\n   197\t┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n   198\t│  📊  Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)    │  ← title bar\n   199\t│                                          [⤢ expand] │\n   200\t├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n   201\t│                                                 │\n   202\t│         [rendered chart / graph here]           │\n   203\t│                                                 │\n   204\t└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n   205\t```\n   206\t\n   207\t- **Title bar:** `#F7F9FC` background, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`. Icon prefix (📊 for charts, 📋 for tables, 🗺 for maps). Title in IBM Plex Sans 13px `#0F1F2E`. Expand button `[⤢]` opens the artifact full-screen in a Modal (`aria-modal=\"true\"`).\n   208\t- **Content area:** White background, `min-height: 200px`, `max-height: 480px`, overflow hidden (scrollable if content exceeds). For iframes, set `width: 100%`, `height: auto` via ResizeObserver.\n   209\t- **Error state:** If parsing or rendering fails, show a bordered `#FFF0F2` panel with icon ⚠, message \"Chart could not be rendered\", and the `description` text if available.\n   210\t- **Loading state:** Skeleton shimmer while the parser/transpiler is working.\n   211\t- **Accessibility:** `role=\"figure\"`, `aria-label={artifact.title}`. If `description` is present, include a visually hidden `<figcaption>` for screen readers. Expand button: `aria-label=\"Expand chart: {title}\"`.\n   212\t\n   213\t#### 5.5.4 Expand / full-screen Modal\n   214\t\n   215\t- Triggered by the `[⤢]` button or keyboard `Enter`/`Space` on it.\n   216\t- MUI `<Dialog fullWidth maxWidth=\"lg\">`, `aria-modal=\"true\"`, `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title.\n   217\t- Focus is trapped inside the modal while open. `Escape` closes it and returns focus to the expand button.\n   218\t- Modal renders the same artifact at full width (re-uses the same parsed/transpiled output — no second parse).\n   219\t\n   220\t### 5.6 ThinkingIndicator\n"
  }
}
