## Entry 193
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:41:56.599Z
**Role/Type:** event_msg
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:41:56.599Z",
  "type": "event_msg",
  "payload": {
    "type": "agent_message",
    "message": "I mean `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md` still describes the chat API as a streaming SSE API, but you said you do not want SSE for v1.\n\nSo the spec currently says this in `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:253`:\n- `POST /api/chat`\n- response is `text/event-stream`\n- frontend handles `token`, `risk_card`, `artifact`, `done`, `error`\n\nThat is the part I mean by “current chat contract”.\n\nSince you want a non-streaming v1, that section should be rewritten to describe a normal JSON response instead.\n\n**Where to replace it**\nUpdate these parts in `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md`:\n\n1. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:253`\n- Section `## 6. API Contract`\n- Replace the SSE response example with a plain JSON response\n\n2. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:278`\n- Remove the list of SSE event types:\n  - `token`\n  - `risk_card`\n  - `artifact`\n  - `done`\n  - `error`\n\n3. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:333`\n- `## 8. State Management`\n- Remove `isStreaming`, `abortController`, and streaming-oriented message accumulation if you are not streaming in v1\n\n4. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:379`\n- `useChat.ts` description currently says SSE streaming logic\n- Change that to simple request/response chat logic\n\n5. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:504`\n- Backend route responsibilities currently say “stream the SSE response back to the client”\n- Change that to “return a JSON chat response”\n\n6. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:516`\n- `agent_proxy.stream_chat()` is streaming-specific\n- Replace with something like `agent_proxy.get_chat_response()`\n\n7. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:553` onward\n- Implementation order mentions SSE and artifact event handling\n- rewrite those steps for plain JSON flow\n\n**What it should become**\nInstead of this idea:\n\n```text\nfrontend -> POST /api/chat -> receives many SSE events\n```\n\nyou want this:\n\n```text\nfrontend -> POST /api/chat -> receives one JSON response\n```\n\nExample v1 response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"message\": \"Based on recent rainfall and drainage reports, Sarjapur Road has elevated flood risk tonight.\",\n  \"artifact\": {\n    \"type\": \"html\",\n    \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend - Sarjapur Road\",\n    \"source\": \"<div>...</div>\",\n    \"description\": \"Rainfall trend for the last 7 days\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\nOr without artifact:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"message\": \"Current AQI in Bellandur is moderate, with no severe health advisory.\",\n  \"artifact\": null\n}\n```\n\n**Why this matters**\nRight now the spec says:\n- the frontend must assemble messages incrementally from stream events\n\nBut your intended v1 says:\n- the frontend will just wait for one response and render it\n\nThose are two different frontend architectures. If the spec keeps SSE language, the implementer will build the wrong thing.\n\nSo in short:\n- “chat contract” = the API request/response shape described in the spec\n- “replace it” = edit `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md` so `/api/chat` returns one JSON object, not SSE events\n\nIf you want, I can rewrite just those sections for you in clear replacement text.",
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

If the spec keeps SSE language, the implementer will build the wrong thing.\n\nSo in short:\n- “chat contract” = the API request/response shape described in the spec\n- “replace it” = edit `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md` so `/api/chat` returns one JSON object, not SSE events\n\nIf you want, I can rewrite just those sections for you in clear replacement text.",

API Contract`\n- Replace the SSE response example with a plain JSON response\n\n2. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:278`\n- Remove the list of SSE event types:\n  - `token`\n  - `risk_card`\n  - `artifact`\n  - `done`\n  - `error`\n\n3. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:333`\n- `## 8. State Management`\n- Remove `isStreaming`, `abortController`, and streaming-oriented message accumulation if you are not streaming in v1\n\n4. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:379`\n- `useChat.ts` description currently says SSE streaming logic\n- Change that to simple request/response chat logic\n\n5. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:504`\n- Backend route responsibilities currently say “stream the SSE response back to the client”\n- Change that to “return a JSON chat response”\n\n6. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:516`\n- `agent_proxy.stream_chat()` is streaming-specific\n- Replace with something like `agent_proxy.get_chat_response()`\n\n7. `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md:553` onward\n- Implementation order mentions SSE and artifact event handling\n- rewrite those steps for plain JSON flow\n\n**What it should become**\nInstead of this idea:\n\n```text\nfrontend -> POST /api/chat -> receives many SSE events\n```\n\nyou want this:\n\n```text\nfrontend -> POST /api/chat -> receives one JSON response\n```\n\nExample v1 response:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"message\": \"Based on recent rainfall and drainage reports, Sarjapur Road has elevated flood risk tonight.\",\n  \"artifact\": {\n    \"type\": \"html\",\n    \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend - Sarjapur Road\",\n    \"source\": \"<div>...</div>\",\n    \"description\": \"Rainfall trend for the last 7 days\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\nOr without artifact:\n\n```json\n{\n  \"message\": \"Current AQI in Bellandur is moderate, with no severe health advisory.\",\n  \"artifact\": null\n}\n```\n\n**Why this matters**\nRight now the spec says:\n- the frontend must assemble messages incrementally from stream events\n\nBut your intended v1 says:\n- the frontend will just wait for one response and render it\n\nThose are two different frontend architectures. If the spec keeps SSE language, the implementer will build the wrong thing.\n\nSo in short:\n- “chat contract” = the API request/response shape described in the spec\n- “replace it” = edit `docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md` so `/api/chat` returns one JSON object, not SSE events\n\nIf you want, I can rewrite just those sections for you in clear replacement text."
      }
    ],
    "phase": "final_answer"
  }
}
```

        "type": "input_text",
        "text": "<collaboration_mode># Collaboration Mode: Default\n\nYou are now in Default mode. Any previous instructions for other modes (e.g. Plan mode) are no longer active.\n\nYour active mode changes only when new developer instructions with a different `<collaboration_mode>...</collaboration_mode>` change it; user requests or tool descriptions do not change mode by themselves. Known mode names are Default and Plan.\n\n## request_user_input availability\n\nThe `request_user_input` tool is unavailable in Default mode. If you call it while in Default mode, it will return an error.\n\nIn Default mode, strongly prefer making reasonable assumptions and executing the user's request rather than stopping to ask questions. If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n</collaboration_mode>"
      }
    ]
  }
}

If you absolutely must ask a question because the answer cannot be discovered from local context and a reasonable assumption would be risky, ask the user directly with a concise plain-text question. Never write a multiple choice question as a textual assistant message.\n"
      }

    "effort": "medium",
    "summary": "none",
    "user_instructions": "## Skills\nA skill is a set of local instructions to follow that is stored in a `SKILL.md` file. Below is the list of skills that can be used. Each entry includes a name, description, and file path so you can open the source for full instructions when using a specific skill.\n### Available skills\n- find-skills: Helps users discover and install agent skills when they ask questions like \"how do I do X\", \"find a skill for X\", \"is there a skill that can...\", or express interest in extending capabilities. This skill should be used when the user is looking for functionality that might exist as an installable skill. (file: /home/dell/.agents/skills/find-skills/SKILL.md)\n- skill-creator: Guide for creating effective skills. This skill should be used when users want to create a new skill (or update an existing skill) that extends Codex's capabilities with specialized knowledge, workflows, or tool integrations. (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-creator/SKILL.md)\n- skill-installer: Install Codex skills into $CODEX_HOME/skills from a curated list or a GitHub repo path. Use when a user asks to list installable skills, install a curated skill, or install a skill from another repo (including private repos). (file: /home/dell/.codex/skills/.system/skill-installer/SKILL.md)\n### How to use skills\n- Discovery: The list above is the skills available in this session (name + description + file path). Skill bodies live on disk at the listed paths.\n- Trigger rules: If the user names a skill (with `$SkillName` or plain text) OR the task clearly matches a skill's description shown above, you must use that skill for that turn. Multiple mentions mean use them all. Do not carry skills across turns unless re-mentioned.\n- Missing/blocked: If a named skill isn't in the list or the path can't be read, say so briefly and continue with the best fallback.\n- How to use a skill (progressive disclosure):\n  1) After deciding to use a skill, open its `SKILL.md`. Read only enough to follow the workflow.\n  2) When `SKILL.md` references relative paths (e.g., `scripts/foo.py`), resolve them relative to the skill directory listed above first, and only consider other paths if needed.\n  3) If `SKILL.md` points to extra folders such as `references/`, load only the specific files needed for the request; don't bulk-load everything.\n  4) If `scripts/` exist, prefer running or patching them instead of retyping large code blocks.\n  5) If `assets/` or templates exist, reuse them instead of recreating from scratch.\n- Coordination and sequencing:\n  - If multiple skills apply, choose the minimal set that covers the request and state the order you'll use them.\n  - Announce which skill(s) you're using and why (one short line). If you skip an obvious skill, say why.\n- Context hygiene:\n  - Keep context small: summarize long sections instead of pasting them; only load extra files when needed.\n  - Avoid deep reference-chasing: prefer opening only files directly linked from `SKILL.md` unless you're blocked.\n  - When variants exist (frameworks, providers, domains), pick only the relevant reference file(s) and note that choice.\n- Safety and fallback: If a skill can't be applied cleanly (missing files, unclear instructions), state the issue, pick the next-best approach, and continue.",
    "truncation_policy": {
      "mode": "tokens",
      "limit": 10000
    }
  }
}
```

## Entry 211
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:43:48.757Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:43:48.757Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_e2EUuqMjtLqTGak43WUhJGqo",
    "output": "Chunk ID: ae43ac\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 1254\nOutput:\n\nHEADER ## 2. Architecture at 2076\n## 2. Architecture\n\n```\nUser (Browser)\n    │\n    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n    ▼\nFastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n    │\n    │  in-process call (Python import, not HTTP)\n    ▼\nAgent Server  (agent/)              ← same repo, same Docker Compose\n    │\n    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, Redis, sensor APIs\n    ▼\nAgent Response  (streamed text + optional structured JSON block + optional artifact HTML/JSX)\n```\n\nThe Agent Server is a **first-party process you own** — ru\n\nHEADER ### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer at 9051\n### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n\nRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event.** The agent produces artifacts when a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation communicates the answer better than prose — for example, a 7-day rainfall trend chart, a neighbourhood AQI heatmap, or a congestion timeline by hour.\n\n#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n\nThe `artifact` SSE payload carries a self-contained string of either HTML or JSX. The agent sets a `type` field \n\nHEADER ## 6. API Contract at 15920\n## 6. API Contract\n\n### POST `/api/chat`\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response (streaming — `text/event-stream` SSE):**\n```\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \"Based on the 2022 flood post-mortem\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \" and current drainage capacity...\"}\ndata: {\"type\": \"risk_card\", \"payload\": { ...structured risk JSON... }}\ndata: {\"type\": \"done\"}\n\n\nHEADER ## 8. State Management at 19512\n## 8. State Management\n\nNo external state library needed. Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n\n```typescript\n// ChatPage state\nconst [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\nconst [input, setInput] = useState('');\nconst [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);\nconst [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'error'>('connecting');\nconst sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\nconst abortController = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n```\n\n```typescript\n\nHEADER ## 9. File Structure at 20742\n## 9. File Structure\n\n```\nclient/src/\n├── App.tsx                        # Single route → ChatPage\n├── main.tsx                       # Providers (ThemeProvider, etc.)\n├── lib/\n│   └── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold) + SSE helper\n├── hooks/\n│   ├── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send, stream, abort, session\n│   └── useArtifactParser.ts       # Parses/transpiles ArtifactPayload → renderable output\n├── components/\n│   ├── layout/\n│   │   └── Header.t\n\nHEADER ## 11. Environment Variables at 25348\n## 11. Environment Variables\n\nAdd to the scaffold's existing env var pattern:\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agent:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Only used if agent runs as a separate process; omit if in-process. |\n| `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | *(proxied via Vite → :8010)* | Frontend API base (already handled by Vite proxy in scaffold) |\n\n---\n\n## 12. Backend Route to Add\n\nAdd one route to `serv\n\nHEADER ## 12. Backend Route to Add at 25797\n## 12. Backend Route to Add\n\nAdd one route to `server/app/api/routes/`:\n\n```\nserver/app/api/routes/chat.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.stream_chat()`.\n- Stream the SSE response back to the client.\n- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state.\n\nAdd one service:\n\n```\nserver/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- `async def stream_chat(session_id, message) → AsyncIter\n\nHEADER ## 13. Out of Scope (Not in this spec) at 26888\n## 13. Out of Scope (Not in this spec)\n\n- Authentication / user accounts.\n- Chat history persistence (no DB writes for chat messages).\n- Map rendering (separate concern — existing MetroSense dashboard).\n- Mobile-specific layout (responsive is sufficient — no native app).\n- Multi-agent routing or tool call visibility in the UI.\n- **Keyword-based or client-side RiskCard triggering.** The frontend must never inspect message content to decide whether to show a RiskCard. That decision belongs entirel\n\nHEADER ## 14. Implementation Order at 28201\n## 14. Implementation Order\n\n1. `theme/metrosense.ts` — get the design tokens right first.\n2. `Header.tsx` + `StatusPill` — static shell.\n3. `types/chat.ts` — lock the interfaces including `ArtifactPayload`.\n4. `useChat.ts` hook — SSE streaming logic (mock the backend with a local stub first); handle `artifact` event alongside `risk_card` and `token`.\n5. `InputBar.tsx` + `SuggestedPrompts.tsx`.\n6. `MessageBubble.tsx` + `ThinkingIndicator.tsx`.\n7. `RiskCard.tsx`.\n8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOM\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 213
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:44:12.790Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:44:12.790Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_GSLwnhcqStku6IsKmS7Ip1Z7",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n-```\n-User (Browser)\n-    │\n-    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n-    ▼\n-FastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n-    │\n-    │  in-process call (Python import, not HTTP)\n-    ▼\n-Agent Server  (agent/)              ← same repo, same Docker Compose\n-    │\n-    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, Redis, sensor APIs\n-    ▼\n-Agent Response  (streamed text + optional structured JSON block + optional artifact HTML/JSX)\n-```\n-\n-The Agent Server is a **first-party process you own** — running in the same Docker Compose stack. The FastAPI backend calls it **in-process** (direct Python import) or over localhost if you choose to run it as a separate uvicorn process on a different port (e.g. `:8020`). Either way, the frontend never calls it directly — all chat traffic goes through `POST /api/chat` on the FastAPI backend.\n+```\n+User (Browser)\n+    │\n+    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n+    ▼\n+FastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n+    │\n+    │  internal HTTP call\n+    ▼\n+Agent Server  (agents/)            ← internal service, e.g. port 8020\n+    │\n+    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, sensor APIs, local knowledge sources\n+    ▼\n+Agent Response  (message text + optional RiskCard JSON + optional sanitized HTML artifact)\n+```\n+\n+The Agent Server is a **first-party internal service you own**. For v1, the architecture is fixed as `frontend -> backend -> agents`, with the frontend never calling the agent server directly. The FastAPI backend remains the only browser-facing API boundary and is responsible for validation, orchestration, error handling, and returning a stable response contract to the UI.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 216
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:44:57.969Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:44:57.969Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_SUOWyb4p1V1e9Nn7OrPsVN91",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n-### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n-\n-Rendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event.** The agent produces artifacts when a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation communicates the answer better than prose — for example, a 7-day rainfall trend chart, a neighbourhood AQI heatmap, or a congestion timeline by hour.\n-\n-#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n-\n-The `artifact` SSE payload carries a self-contained string of either HTML or JSX. The agent sets a `type` field to indicate which:\n-\n-```typescript\n-interface ArtifactPayload {\n-  type: \"html\" | \"jsx\";\n-  title: string;                 // e.g. \"Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)\"\n-  source: string;                // the raw HTML or JSX string\n-  description?: string;          // optional plain-text alt description for screen readers\n-}\n-```\n-\n-Examples of artifacts the agent might produce:\n-- A Recharts `<LineChart>` of hourly rainfall over the past 48 hours\n-- An HTML `<table>` of neighbourhood AQI readings ranked by severity\n-- A `<BarChart>` of historical flood incidents per zone\n-- An SVG choropleth of traffic congestion across Bengaluru\n-\n-#### 5.5.2 Parser (`useArtifactParser` hook)\n-\n-The hook lives at `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts`. It receives an `ArtifactPayload` and returns a resolved, safe render target.\n-\n-**For `type: \"html\"`:**\n-1. Receive the raw HTML string from the agent.\n-2. Sanitise with DOMPurify (strip `<script>`, event handlers, external resource URLs) — never trust agent-produced markup directly.\n-3. Render inside a sandboxed `<iframe srcdoc={sanitisedHtml} sandbox=\"allow-scripts allow-same-origin\" />`. The iframe must have `title={artifact.title}` for screen readers.\n-4. The iframe auto-sizes to fit its content via a `ResizeObserver` posted message from inside the iframe.\n-\n-**For `type: \"jsx\"`:**\n-1. Receive the JSX string. This is expected to be a self-contained Recharts / Victory / plain React component — no imports, no external fetches.\n-2. Use `@babel/standalone` to transpile the JSX string to plain JS at runtime (already bundled — no network call).\n-3. Evaluate the transpiled JS in a controlled scope that provides only: `React`, `ReactDOM`, and a pre-approved chart library whitelist (`recharts`, `victory`, `d3` — nothing else). Evaluation uses `new Function(...)` with an explicit scope object — never `eval()` directly.\n-4. Render the resulting React element into a dedicated `<div>` inside a `React.Suspense` boundary with an error boundary wrapping it.\n-5. On any parse or render error, fall back to the `description` field as plain text, or a generic \"Chart could not be rendered\" message if no description is present.\n-\n-**Security rules (non-negotiable):**\n-- HTML artifacts: DOMPurify sanitisation before any insertion into the DOM.\n-- JSX artifacts: only the approved library scope is exposed to the evaluated code — no `window`, no `document`, no `fetch`.\n-- Neither artifact type may load external URLs (network requests, images from remote origins, fonts).\n-- Content Security Policy header on the app must include `sandbox` restrictions covering artifact iframes.\n-\n-#### 5.5.3 ArtifactRenderer component\n+### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n+\n+Rendered inline within an assistant message **only when the backend returns an `artifact` object** in the chat response. For v1, the agent may return **HTML artifacts only**. No JSX, runtime code execution, or client-side chart evaluation is part of this spec.\n+\n+#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n+\n+The backend forwards an artifact payload with a fixed HTML-only shape:\n+\n+```typescript\n+interface ArtifactPayload {\n+  type: \"html\";\n+  title: string;                 // e.g. \"Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)\"\n+  source: string;                // raw HTML string from the agent service\n+  description?: string;          // optional plain-text alt description for screen readers\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Examples of artifacts the agent might produce:\n+- An HTML `<table>` of neighborhood AQI readings ranked by severity\n+- An inline SVG rainfall trend chart\n+- A compact HTML risk-comparison panel across locations\n+\n+#### 5.5.2 Sanitization and sandboxing\n+\n+The renderer receives an `ArtifactPayload` and converts it into a safe render target.\n+\n+1. Receive the raw HTML string from the backend.\n+2. Sanitize it with DOMPurify before any rendering.\n+3. Render the sanitized markup inside a sandboxed `<iframe srcDoc={sanitizedHtml} />`.\n+4. Keep the iframe isolated from the main app so agent-generated markup cannot affect app styles, script execution, or global state.\n+\n+**Allowed artifact content for v1:**\n+- plain HTML elements such as `div`, `span`, `p`, `table`, `thead`, `tbody`, `tr`, `td`, `th`, `ul`, `li`\n+- inline SVG for charts\n+- inline styles only if explicitly allowed by the sanitizer configuration\n+\n+**Disallowed artifact content for v1:**\n+- `<script>`\n+- inline event handlers such as `onclick`\n+- external resource URLs\n+- remote images, fonts, or stylesheets\n+- nested iframes\n+- arbitrary JavaScript execution\n+\n+**Security rules (non-negotiable):**\n+- HTML artifacts must be sanitized before insertion into `srcDoc`.\n+- The iframe sandbox must stay strict; do not combine permissive flags such as `allow-scripts` and `allow-same-origin` for agent-generated content.\n+- If sanitization strips most or all of the content, fall back to a safe error state plus the optional `description`.\n+\n+#### 5.5.3 ArtifactRenderer component\n@@\n-- **Content area:** White background, `min-height: 200px`, `max-height: 480px`, overflow hidden (scrollable if content exceeds). For iframes, set `width: 100%`, `height: auto` via ResizeObserver.\n+- **Content area:** White background, `min-height: 200px`, `max-height: 480px`, overflow hidden (scrollable if content exceeds). For iframes, set `width: 100%` and auto-size height to the sanitized content.\n - **Error state:** If parsing or rendering fails, show a bordered `#FFF0F2` panel with icon ⚠, message \"Chart could not be rendered\", and the `description` text if available.\n-- **Loading state:** Skeleton shimmer while the parser/transpiler is working.\n+- **Loading state:** Skeleton shimmer while sanitization completes.\n - **Accessibility:** `role=\"figure\"`, `aria-label={artifact.title}`. If `description` is present, include a visually hidden `<figcaption>` for screen readers. Expand button: `aria-label=\"Expand chart: {title}\"`.\n@@\n-- Modal renders the same artifact at full width (re-uses the same parsed/transpiled output — no second parse).\n+- Modal renders the same sanitized artifact at full width.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 219
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:45:31.939Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:45:31.939Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_qGgzx7YaDmEBgB03KvTrltLj",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n-## 6. API Contract\n-\n-### POST `/api/chat`\n-\n-**Request:**\n-```json\n-{\n-  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n-  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n-}\n-```\n-\n-**Response (streaming — `text/event-stream` SSE):**\n-```\n-data: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \"Based on the 2022 flood post-mortem\"}\n-data: {\"type\": \"token\", \"content\": \" and current drainage capacity...\"}\n-data: {\"type\": \"risk_card\", \"payload\": { ...structured risk JSON... }}\n-data: {\"type\": \"done\"}\n-```\n-\n-**Error response:**\n-```json\n-{ \"error\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\", \"message\": \"...\" }\n-```\n-\n-The frontend handles five SSE event types:\n-- `token` — append content to the current assistant bubble's text.\n-- `risk_card` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `riskCard` and render a RiskCard below the streamed text. The frontend must never emit or synthesize this event itself. A single response can contain both `token` events and one `risk_card` event. The `risk_card` event may arrive before, during, or after the token stream — the frontend must handle all orderings gracefully.\n-- `artifact` — **agent-emitted only.** Attach the payload to the current message as `artifact` and render an `ArtifactRenderer` below any RiskCard. Payload schema: `{ type: \"html\" | \"jsx\", title: string, source: string, description?: string }`. The frontend must sanitise/sandbox the content before rendering (see §5.5). A response may contain at most one `artifact` event; if multiple arrive, render only the last one and log a warning.\n-- `done` — mark message complete, re-enable input.\n-- `error` — show inline error banner in the message bubble.\n-\n-**GET `/api/health`** — used by StatusPill to poll agent connectivity (poll every 30s).\n+## 6. API Contract\n+\n+### POST `/api/chat`\n+\n+**Request:**\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n+  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+**Response (`application/json`):**\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"message\": \"Based on drainage capacity reports and recent rainfall patterns, Sarjapur Road has elevated flood risk tonight.\",\n+  \"risk_card\": {\n+    \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n+    \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n+    \"overall_risk_score\": 9.1\n+  },\n+  \"artifact\": {\n+    \"type\": \"html\",\n+    \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend - Sarjapur Road\",\n+    \"source\": \"<div>...</div>\",\n+    \"description\": \"Seven-day rainfall trend for Sarjapur Road\"\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+`risk_card` and `artifact` are both optional. Most responses will return `message` text only.\n+\n+**Error response:**\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"error\": {\n+    \"code\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n+    \"message\": \"The agent service is currently unavailable.\"\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+**GET `/api/health`** — used by `StatusPill` to poll composite system health every 30 seconds.\n+\n+Recommended response shape:\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n+  \"agent\": \"ok\",\n+  \"status\": \"online\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Example degraded response:\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n+  \"agent\": \"down\",\n+  \"status\": \"degraded\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+The frontend behavior should be:\n+- render `message` as the assistant reply\n+- render `risk_card` only when the response includes it\n+- render `artifact` only when the response includes it\n+- show degraded state when backend is reachable but agent health is not `ok`\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

Most responses will return `message` text only.\n+\n+**Error response:**\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"error\": {\n+    \"code\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n+    \"message\": \"The agent service is currently unavailable.\"\n+  }\n+}\n+```\n+\n+**GET `/api/health`** — used by `StatusPill` to poll composite system health every 30 seconds.\n+\n+Recommended response shape:\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n+  \"agent\": \"ok\",\n+  \"status\": \"online\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+Example degraded response:\n+```json\n+{\n+  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n+  \"agent\": \"down\",\n+  \"status\": \"degraded\"\n+}\n+```\n+\n+The frontend behavior should be:\n+- render `message` as the assistant reply\n+- render `risk_card` only when the response includes it\n+- render `artifact` only when the response includes it\n+- show degraded state when backend is reachable but agent health is not `ok`\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

## Entry 222
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:45:40.252Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:45:40.252Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_ukEB1hgHWkbLHj3VIqjAcMdt",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n ## 8. State Management\n \n No external state library needed. Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n \n ```typescript\n // ChatPage state\n const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\n const [input, setInput] = useState('');\n-const [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);\n+const [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);\n const [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'error'>('connecting');\n const sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\n-const abortController = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n ```\n \n ```typescript\n interface ArtifactPayload {\n-  type: 'html' | 'jsx';\n+  type: 'html';\n   title: string;\n-  source: string;              // raw HTML or JSX string from the agent\n+  source: string;              // raw HTML string from the agent\n   description?: string;        // plain-text alt for screen readers / error fallback\n }\n \n interface Message {\n   id: string;\n   role: 'user' | 'assistant';\n-  content: string;             // accumulated text\n+  content: string;\n   riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;  // optional structured block\n   artifact?: ArtifactPayload;  // optional chart / visualisation artifact\n   timestamp: Date;\n-  isStreaming?: boolean;\n+  isError?: boolean;\n }\n ```\n \n-Artifact parse state (parsing/transpiling is async) is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n+Artifact sanitization state is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n \n ```typescript\n // ChatPage state\n const [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\n const [input, setInput] = useState('');\n-const [isStreaming, setIsStreaming] = useState(false);\n+const [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);\n const [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'error'>('connecting');\n const sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\n-const abortController = useRef<AbortController | null>(null);\n ```\n \n ```typescript\n interface ArtifactPayload {\n-  type: 'html' | 'jsx';\n+  type: 'html';\n   title: string;\n-  source: string;              // raw HTML or JSX string from the agent\n+  source: string;              // raw HTML string from the agent\n   description?: string;        // plain-text alt for screen readers / error fallback\n }\n \n interface Message {\n   id: string;\n   role: 'user' | 'assistant';\n-  content: string;             // accumulated text\n+  content: string;\n   riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;  // optional structured block\n   artifact?: ArtifactPayload;  // optional chart / visualisation artifact\n   timestamp: Date;\n-  isStreaming?: boolean;\n+  isError?: boolean;\n }\n ```\n \n-Artifact parse state (parsing/transpiling is async) is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n+Artifact sanitization state is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n*** End Patch\n"

}

## Entry 225
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:45:51.871Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:45:51.871Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_nEjsS93DN8s2KuORLJERTneW",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n client/src/\n ├── App.tsx                        # Single route → ChatPage\n ├── main.tsx                       # Providers (ThemeProvider, etc.)\n ├── lib/\n-│   └── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold) + SSE helper\n+│   ├── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold)\n+│   └── artifactSandbox.ts         # DOMPurify wrapper + iframe srcDoc helpers\n ├── hooks/\n-│   ├── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send, stream, abort, session\n-│   └── useArtifactParser.ts       # Parses/transpiles ArtifactPayload → renderable output\n+│   └── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send request, map response, manage session\n ├── components/\n │   ├── layout/\n │   │   └── Header.tsx\n │   ├── chat/\n │   │   ├── MessageList.tsx\n │   │   ├── MessageBubble.tsx\n │   │   ├── RiskCard.tsx\n-│   │   ├── ArtifactRenderer.tsx   # Sandboxed iframe (HTML) or evaluated JSX renderer\n-│   │   ├── ThinkingIndicator.tsx\n+│   │   ├── ArtifactRenderer.tsx   # Sandboxed iframe for sanitized HTML artifacts\n+│   │   ├── ThinkingIndicator.tsx\n │   │   └── SuggestedPrompts.tsx\n │   └── input/\n │       └── InputBar.tsx\n ├── pages/\n │   └── ChatPage.tsx\n ├── types/\n │   └── chat.ts                    # Message, RiskCardPayload, ArtifactPayload interfaces\n-├── lib/\n-│   └── artifactSandbox.ts         # DOMPurify sanitiser + @babel/standalone JSX transpiler wrapper\n └── theme/\n     └── metrosense.ts              # MUI theme overrides\n ```\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n | Variable | Default | Description |\n |----------|---------|-------------|\n-| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agent:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Only used if agent runs as a separate process; omit if in-process. |\n+| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agents:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Used by the backend to call the internal agent service. |\n | `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | *(proxied via Vite → :8010)* | Frontend API base (already handled by Vite proxy in scaffold) |\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}

## Entry 231
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:46:20.681Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:46:20.681Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_FE0OreVNoRWF3LN0hIDwYNWs",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n Responsibilities:\n - Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n-- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.stream_chat()`.\n-- Stream the SSE response back to the client.\n-- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state.\n+- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.get_chat_response()`.\n+- Return a single JSON response with `message`, optional `risk_card`, and optional `artifact`.\n+- Accept `GET /api/health` and return composite backend + agent health for the frontend status pill.\n+- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state if session reset is implemented.\n@@\n Responsibilities:\n-- `async def stream_chat(session_id, message) → AsyncIterator[str]`\n-- **If Agent Server runs in-process:** import and call it directly — no HTTP overhead.\n-- **If Agent Server runs as a separate process on localhost:** call `http://localhost:8020/chat` (internal only, never crosses the internet).\n-- Handles timeout (30s), connection errors → emits `{\"type\": \"error\", ...}` on failure.\n+- `async def get_chat_response(session_id, message) -> ChatResponse`\n+- Call the internal `agents/` service over HTTP at `AGENT_SERVER_URL`.\n+- Normalize agent failures into backend-owned error responses.\n+- Handle timeout (30s) and connection errors cleanly.\n@@\n-agent:\n-  build: ./agent          # your Agent Server directory\n+agents:\n+  build: ./agents         # your Agent Server directory\n   ports:\n-    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal only; not exposed if in-process\n+    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal service port\n   depends_on:\n     - db\n   environment:\n     - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}\n ```\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_FE0OreVNoRWF3LN0hIDwYNWs",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n Responsibilities:\n - Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n-- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.stream_chat()`.\n-- Stream the SSE response back to the client.\n-- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state.\n+- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.get_chat_response()`.\n+- Return a single JSON response with `message`, optional `risk_card`, and optional `artifact`.\n+- Accept `GET /api/health` and return composite backend + agent health for the frontend status pill.\n+- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state if session reset is implemented.\n@@\n Responsibilities:\n-- `async def stream_chat(session_id, message) → AsyncIterator[str]`\n-- **If Agent Server runs in-process:** import and call it directly — no HTTP overhead.\n-- **If Agent Server runs as a separate process on localhost:** call `http://localhost:8020/chat` (internal only, never crosses the internet).\n-- Handles timeout (30s), connection errors → emits `{\"type\": \"error\", ...}` on failure.\n+- `async def get_chat_response(session_id, message) -> ChatResponse`\n+- Call the internal `agents/` service over HTTP at `AGENT_SERVER_URL`.\n+- Normalize agent failures into backend-owned error responses.\n+- Handle timeout (30s) and connection errors cleanly.\n@@\n-agent:\n-  build: ./agent          # your Agent Server directory\n+agents:\n+  build: ./agents         # your Agent Server directory\n   ports:\n-    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal only; not exposed if in-process\n+    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal service port\n   depends_on:\n     - db\n   environment:\n     - DATABASE_URL=${DATABASE_URL}\n ```\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 234
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:46:35.634Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:46:35.634Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_C36QbR7VYta37IyByrnQv1ZE",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n - Authentication / user accounts.\n - Chat history persistence (no DB writes for chat messages).\n - Map rendering (separate concern — existing MetroSense dashboard).\n - Mobile-specific layout (responsive is sufficient — no native app).\n - Multi-agent routing or tool call visibility in the UI.\n - **Keyword-based or client-side RiskCard triggering.** The frontend must never inspect message content to decide whether to show a RiskCard. That decision belongs entirely to the agent.\n-- **Artifact execution beyond the approved library whitelist.** The JSX artifact parser exposes only `React`, `ReactDOM`, `recharts`, `victory`, and `d3`. Any artifact requiring other libraries (e.g. Mapbox, Leaflet, Plotly) is out of scope for the inline renderer — the agent should not produce such artifacts. If it does, `ArtifactRenderer` must show the error state, not attempt execution.\n-- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers only parsing and rendering on the frontend. How the agent decides to produce an artifact (and what chart library it uses) is defined in the agent spec, not here.\n-- **Multi-artifact messages.** A single agent response renders at most one artifact. If the agent sends multiple `artifact` events in one response, only the last is rendered.\n+- **Executable artifacts.** JSX, arbitrary JavaScript, dynamic chart library evaluation, and client-side code generation are out of scope for v1.\n+- **External resource loading inside artifacts.** No remote fonts, images, stylesheets, scripts, or nested frames.\n+- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers parsing, sanitization, and rendering on the frontend, not how the agent decides to produce the artifact.\n+- **Streaming chat responses.** V1 chat is request/response JSON only.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**
```json
{
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_C36QbR7VYta37IyByrnQv1ZE",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n - Authentication / user accounts.\n - Chat history persistence (no DB writes for chat messages).\n - Map rendering (separate concern — existing MetroSense dashboard).\n - Mobile-specific layout (responsive is sufficient — no native app).\n - Multi-agent routing or tool call visibility in the UI.\n - **Keyword-based or client-side RiskCard triggering.** The frontend must never inspect message content to decide whether to show a RiskCard. That decision belongs entirely to the agent.\n-- **Artifact execution beyond the approved library whitelist.** The JSX artifact parser exposes only `React`, `ReactDOM`, `recharts`, `victory`, and `d3`. Any artifact requiring other libraries (e.g. Mapbox, Leaflet, Plotly) is out of scope for the inline renderer — the agent should not produce such artifacts. If it does, `ArtifactRenderer` must show the error state, not attempt execution.\n-- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers only parsing and rendering on the frontend. How the agent decides to produce an artifact (and what chart library it uses) is defined in the agent spec, not here.\n-- **Multi-artifact messages.** A single agent response renders at most one artifact. If the agent sends multiple `artifact` events in one response, only the last is rendered.\n+- **Executable artifacts.** JSX, arbitrary JavaScript, dynamic chart library evaluation, and client-side code generation are out of scope for v1.\n+- **External resource loading inside artifacts.** No remote fonts, images, stylesheets, scripts, or nested frames.\n+- **Agent-side artifact generation logic.** This spec covers parsing, sanitization, and rendering on the frontend, not how the agent decides to produce the artifact.\n+- **Streaming chat responses.** V1 chat is request/response JSON only.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 237
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:47:06.328Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:47:06.328Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "custom_tool_call",
    "status": "completed",
    "call_id": "call_u6wYRn89Inz30N7yYmkaqMMc",
    "name": "apply_patch",
    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n 1. `theme/metrosense.ts` — get the design tokens right first.\n 2. `Header.tsx` + `StatusPill` — static shell.\n 3. `types/chat.ts` — lock the interfaces including `ArtifactPayload`.\n-4. `useChat.ts` hook — SSE streaming logic (mock the backend with a local stub first); handle `artifact` event alongside `risk_card` and `token`.\n+4. `useChat.ts` hook — request/response chat logic with loading, error, and session handling.\n 5. `InputBar.tsx` + `SuggestedPrompts.tsx`.\n 6. `MessageBubble.tsx` + `ThinkingIndicator.tsx`.\n 7. `RiskCard.tsx`.\n-8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper + `@babel/standalone` JSX transpiler; write unit tests for the sanitiser and the scope-limited evaluator before wiring into the UI.\n-9. `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts` — async hook that takes an `ArtifactPayload` and returns `{ status: 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error', element: ReactNode | null, error?: string }`.\n-10. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n-11. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n-12. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n-13. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration.\n+8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper and iframe `srcDoc` helper; write sanitizer tests before wiring into the UI.\n+9. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n+10. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n+11. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n+12. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration using JSON responses and composite health.\n*** End Patch\n"
  }
}
```
**Metadata:**

    "input": "*** Begin Patch\n*** Update File: docs/FRONTEND_SPEC.md\n@@\n 1. `theme/metrosense.ts` — get the design tokens right first.\n 2. `Header.tsx` + `StatusPill` — static shell.\n 3. `types/chat.ts` — lock the interfaces including `ArtifactPayload`.\n-4. `useChat.ts` hook — SSE streaming logic (mock the backend with a local stub first); handle `artifact` event alongside `risk_card` and `token`.\n+4. `useChat.ts` hook — request/response chat logic with loading, error, and session handling.\n 5. `InputBar.tsx` + `SuggestedPrompts.tsx`.\n 6. `MessageBubble.tsx` + `ThinkingIndicator.tsx`.\n 7. `RiskCard.tsx`.\n-8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper + `@babel/standalone` JSX transpiler; write unit tests for the sanitiser and the scope-limited evaluator before wiring into the UI.\n-9. `hooks/useArtifactParser.ts` — async hook that takes an `ArtifactPayload` and returns `{ status: 'loading' | 'ready' | 'error', element: ReactNode | null, error?: string }`.\n-10. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n-11. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n-12. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n-13. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration.\n+8. `lib/artifactSandbox.ts` — DOMPurify wrapper and iframe `srcDoc` helper; write sanitizer tests before wiring into the UI.\n+9. `ArtifactRenderer.tsx` — compose the title bar, sandboxed content area, expand Modal, loading skeleton, and error state.\n+10. `MessageList.tsx` — compose bubbles including `ArtifactRenderer`, auto-scroll logic.\n+11. `ChatPage.tsx` — wire everything together.\n+12. `server/app/api/routes/chat.py` + `agent_proxy.py` — real backend integration using JSON responses and composite health.\n*** End Patch\n"

## Entry 243
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:47:15.689Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:47:15.689Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_qH7tiejqHGbKx2yVeDbRVgMY",
    "output": "Chunk ID: e07da8\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 850\nOutput:\n7:Users interact via natural language. The agent queries a local PostgreSQL database (real-time sensor data: temperature, humidity, wind speed, rainfall, AQI, lake water levels, power outage logs, traffic heatmaps) and retrieves context from local documentation (IMD weather bulletins, BBMP drainage master plans, flood post-mortem reports, urban forest maps, climate resilience whitepapers). The UI is a **plain chat interface** — it shows streamed text responses for the vast majority of queries.\n9:The chat interface has **two structured output types** beyond plain streamed text — both are agent-decided and never client-inferred:\n11:1. **RiskCard** — rendered when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event carrying a neighborhood risk scorecard as structured JSON (flood probability, power outage risk, traffic delay index, health advisory, emergency readiness).\n12:2. **Artifact** — rendered when the agent emits an `artifact` SSE event carrying a self-contained HTML or JSX string representing a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation. The frontend parses and sandboxes this markup and renders it inline in the conversation. The agent produces these when a visual answer (e.g. a rainfall trend chart, an AQI heatmap, a congestion timeline) is more useful than prose.\n14:The frontend never infers, guesses, or keyword-triggers either output type — the SSE event type is the sole rendering signal.\n64:    │   │   ├── RiskCard?         (optional — agent risk_card SSE event)\n65:    │   │   └── ArtifactRenderer? (optional — agent artifact SSE event)\n66:    │   └── ThinkingIndicator  (streaming / loading state)\n106:- **RiskCard rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains a `risk_card` event. The frontend does not inspect message text or keywords — the presence of `riskCard` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition.\n107:- **Artifact rendering:** Only rendered when the SSE stream contains an `artifact` event. The presence of `artifact` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition. Rendered via `ArtifactRenderer` (see §5.5) below the RiskCard if both are present. A single message may contain streamed text, a RiskCard, an Artifact, or any combination.\n111:Rendered inline within an assistant message **only when the agent emits a `risk_card` SSE event.** This is the structured output for a neighborhood risk scorecard. It is never shown for plain conversational responses — not for flood questions, not for AQI questions, not for any keyword match. The agent decides when a scorecard is warranted.\n229:- Disappears as soon as the first streaming token arrives.\n238:- **Abort:** while streaming, button becomes \"Stop\" (square icon, `background: #DC2626`, `aria-label=\"Stop response\"`). Never rely on colour alone — icon + label both change.\n250:> **Note:** Only the last prompt reliably triggers a RiskCard — it explicitly requests a structured scorecard. The others are natural language queries that return streamed text. Chips are for user convenience, not a trigger mechanism.\n328:The agent emits this as a `risk_card` SSE event payload when it determines a structured neighborhood scorecard is the appropriate response. The frontend renders it as a RiskCard component. All top-level metric fields are optional — the agent only populates fields it has sufficient data to compute.\n"
  }
}
```

## Entry 259
**Timestamp:** 2026-03-10T09:48:22.576Z
**Role/Type:** response_item
**Content:**
```json
{
  "timestamp": "2026-03-10T09:48:22.576Z",
  "type": "response_item",
  "payload": {
    "type": "function_call_output",
    "call_id": "call_R7129udL1NikSTJ68wIJnGsp",
    "output": "Chunk ID: 0e13d9\nWall time: 0.0000 seconds\nProcess exited with code 0\nOriginal token count: 5565\nOutput:\n# FRONTEND_SPEC.md — MetroSense Chat Interface\n\n## 1. Overview\n\nA minimal, single-page chat interface for the **UrbanClimate AI** system, serving city planners, BBMP disaster teams, and logistics companies in evaluating weather-related risks and infrastructure readiness for Bengaluru.\n\nUsers interact via natural language. The agent queries a local PostgreSQL database (real-time sensor data: temperature, humidity, wind speed, rainfall, AQI, lake water levels, power outage logs, traffic heatmaps) and retrieves context from local documentation (IMD weather bulletins, BBMP drainage master plans, flood post-mortem reports, urban forest maps, climate resilience whitepapers). The UI is a **plain chat interface** — it shows a text response for the vast majority of queries.\n\nThe chat interface has **two structured output types** beyond plain text — both are agent-decided and never client-inferred:\n\n1. **RiskCard** — rendered when the backend response contains a `risk_card` object carrying a neighborhood risk scorecard as structured JSON (flood probability, power outage risk, traffic delay index, health advisory, emergency readiness).\n2. **Artifact** — rendered when the backend response contains an `artifact` object carrying sanitized HTML for a chart, graph, table, or data visualisation. The frontend sanitizes and sandboxes this markup before rendering it inline in the conversation.\n\nThe frontend never infers, guesses, or keyword-triggers either output type — the presence of structured response fields is the sole rendering signal.\n\n**Design language:** Clean, accessible light theme. Background `#F7F9FC`, white card surfaces, slate-navy `#0F1F2E` text, steel-blue `#005F8E` primary accent. IBM Plex Sans for body copy, IBM Plex Mono for data/labels. All colours meet WCAG 2.1 AA (≥ 4.5:1 for text, ≥ 3:1 for UI components). No colour-only status signals — every state is communicated with colour + icon + text label.\n\n---\n\n## 2. Architecture\n\n```\nUser (Browser)\n    │\n    │  HTTP POST /api/chat  (message + session_id)\n    ▼\nFastAPI Backend  (server/)          ← port 8010\n    │\n    │  internal HTTP call\n    ▼\nAgent Server  (agents/)            ← internal service, e.g. port 8020\n    │\n    │  tool calls → PostgreSQL, sensor APIs, local knowledge sources\n    ▼\nAgent Response  (message text + optional RiskCard JSON + optional sanitized HTML artifact)\n```\n\nThe Agent Server is a **first-party internal service you own**. For v1, the architecture is fixed as `frontend -> backend -> agents`, with the frontend never calling the agent server directly. The FastAPI backend remains the only browser-facing API boundary and is responsible for validation, orchestration, error handling, and returning a stable response contract to the UI.\n\n---\n\n## 3. Pages / Routes\n\n| Route | Component | Description |\n|-------|-----------|-------------|\n| `/` | `ChatPage` | The only page. Full-height chat interface. |\n\nNo routing library needed beyond React Router with a single route, or just a single-page render.\n\n---\n\n## 4. Component Tree\n\n```\nApp\n└── ChatPage\n    ├── Header\n    │   ├── Logo (\"METROSENSE\" + sub-label)\n    │   ├── StatusPill (agent connection status)\n    │   └── SessionId (mono, dimmed)\n    ├── MessageList  (scrollable, flex-col)\n    │   ├── MessageBubble (role: user | assistant)\n    │   │   ├── TextContent       (markdown-rendered)\n    │   │   ├── RiskCard?         (optional — backend `risk_card` payload)\n    │   │   └── ArtifactRenderer? (optional — backend `artifact` payload)\n    │   └── ThinkingIndicator  (request in flight / loading state)\n    └── InputBar\n        ├── TextArea  (auto-resize, max 4 rows)\n        ├── SendButton\n        └── SuggestedPrompts  (shown only when MessageList is empty)\n```\n\n---\n\n## 5. Component Specifications\n\n### 5.1 Header\n\n- Sticky top, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`, `box-shadow: 0 1px 4px rgba(0,0,0,0.06)`.\n- **Logo:** \"METROSENSE\" in IBM Plex Sans 600 20px, colour `#0F1F2E`. Sub-label: \"BENGALURU CLIMATE INTELLIGENCE\" in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`.\n- **StatusPill:** Icon (✓ / spinner / ⚠) + colour tint + text label — never colour alone. States:\n  - Online: `#F0FDF4` background, `#14532D` text, `#16A34A` left border, label \"Agent Online\"\n  - Connecting: `#FFF9EB` background, `#6B4C00` text, `#CA8A04` left border, label \"Connecting…\"\n  - Degraded: `#FFF0F2` background, `#9B1D2A` text, `#DC2626` left border, label \"Degraded\"\n- **SessionId:** `SESSION · <id[:8]>` in IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`. Tap to copy — expose `aria-label=\"Copy session ID\"` and a visible focus ring on the button.\n\n### 5.2 MessageList\n\n- `overflow-y: auto`, flex column, padding `24px 0`, gap between bubbles `16px`.\n- Auto-scrolls to bottom on new messages.\n- Maintains scroll position if user has scrolled up (don't yank them down).\n\n### 5.3 MessageBubble\n\n**User bubble:**\n- Right-aligned.\n- Background: `#EEF2F7`, border: `1px solid #C9D6E3`, border-radius `12px 12px 2px 12px`.\n- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E` (contrast 14.1:1 on `#EEF2F7` — AAA).\n- Timestamp: IBM Plex Mono 11px, colour `#4A5568`, below bubble.\n\n**Assistant bubble:**\n- Left-aligned, slightly wider (max-width 80%).\n- Background: `#FFFFFF`, left border: `3px solid #005F8E`.\n- Text: IBM Plex Sans 14px, colour `#0F1F2E`.\n- Renders Markdown: bold, inline code, bullet lists, code blocks styled with mono font and `#F7F9FC` background with `#D1DBE8` border.\n- **RiskCard rendering:** Only rendered when the response contains a `risk_card` payload. The frontend does not inspect message text or keywords — the presence of `riskCard` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition.\n- **Artifact rendering:** Only rendered when the response contains an `artifact` payload. The presence of `artifact` data on the `Message` object is the sole rendering condition. Rendered via `ArtifactRenderer` (see §5.5) below the RiskCard if both are present. A single message may contain plain text, a RiskCard, an Artifact, or any combination.\n\n### 5.4 RiskCard\n\nRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the backend returns a `risk_card` object.** This is the structured output for a neighborhood risk scorecard. It is never shown for plain conversational responses — not for flood questions, not for AQI questions, not for any keyword match. The agent decides when a scorecard is warranted.\n\nThe card surfaces four risk dimensions derived from the agent's cross-analysis of sensor data and documentation:\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  SARJAPUR ROAD  ·  RISK SCORE: 9.1 ◼    │  ← neighborhood + overall score badge\n├─────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│  FLOOD         ████████░░  94%  CRITICAL │  ← flood_risk (DB rainfall + drainage docs)\n│  POWER OUTAGE  ██████░░░░  72%  HIGH     │  ← power_outage_risk (wind telemetry + outage logs)\n\n---\n\n**What each field represents:**\n- `flood_risk` — agent's cross-reference of predicted rainfall intensity against drainage capacity documents (BBMP master plan, 2022 flood post-mortems) and lake water level telemetry.\n- `power_outage_risk` — probability derived from wind-speed telemetry merged with tree-density maps and historical outage incident logs.\n- `traffic_delay_index` — estimated delay factor from rainfall-threshold/traffic-bottleneck correlation in historical congestion heatmaps.\n- `health_advisory` — AQI sensor reading synthesized with Urban Heat Island research docs; `aqi_category` surfaces the advisory level for sensitive populations.\n- `emergency_readiness` — agent's final recommendation and specific action items (e.g., underpass barricading, evacuation routes).\n\n### 5.5 ArtifactRenderer\n\nRendered inline within an assistant message **only when the backend returns an `artifact` object** in the chat response. For v1, the agent may return **HTML artifacts only**. No JSX, runtime code execution, or client-side chart evaluation is part of this spec.\n\n#### 5.5.1 What the agent sends\n\nThe backend forwards an artifact payload with a fixed HTML-only shape:\n\n```typescript\ninterface ArtifactPayload {\n  type: \"html\";\n  title: string;                 // e.g. \"Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)\"\n  source: string;                // raw HTML string from the agent service\n  description?: string;          // optional plain-text alt description for screen readers\n}\n```\n\nExamples of artifacts the agent might produce:\n- An HTML `<table>` of neighborhood AQI readings ranked by severity\n- An inline SVG rainfall trend chart\n- A compact HTML risk-comparison panel across locations\n\n#### 5.5.2 Sanitization and sandboxing\n\nThe renderer receives an `ArtifactPayload` and converts it into a safe render target.\n\n1. Receive the raw HTML string from the backend.\n2. Sanitize it with DOMPurify before any rendering.\n3. Render the sanitized markup inside a sandboxed `<iframe srcDoc={sanitizedHtml} />`.\n4. Keep the iframe isolated from the main app so agent-generated markup cannot affect app styles, script execution, or global state.\n\n**Allowed artifact content for v1:**\n- plain HTML elements such as `div`, `span`, `p`, `table`, `thead`, `tbody`, `tr`, `td`, `th`, `ul`, `li`\n- inline SVG for charts\n- inline styles only if explicitly allowed by the sanitizer configuration\n\n**Disallowed artifact content for v1:**\n- `<script>`\n- inline event handlers such as `onclick`\n- external resource URLs\n- remote images, fonts, or stylesheets\n- nested iframes\n- arbitrary JavaScript execution\n\n**Security rules (non-negotiable):**\n- HTML artifacts must be sanitized before insertion into `srcDoc`.\n- The iframe sandbox must stay strict; do not combine permissive flags such as `allow-scripts` and `allow-same-origin` for agent-generated content.\n- If sanitization strips most or all of the content, fall back to a safe error state plus the optional `description`.\n\n#### 5.5.3 ArtifactRenderer component\n\n```\n┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────┐\n│  📊  Rainfall Trend — Sarjapur Road (7 days)    │  ← title bar\n│                                          [⤢ expand] │\n├─────────────────────────────────────────────────┤\n│                                                 │\n│         [rendered chart / graph here]           │\n│                                                 │\n└─────────────────────────────────────────────────┘\n```\n\n- **Title bar:** `#F7F9FC` background, `border-bottom: 1px solid #D1DBE8`. Icon prefix (📊 for charts, 📋 for tables, 🗺 for maps). Title in IBM Plex Sans 13px `#0F1F2E`. Expand button `[⤢]` opens the artifact full-screen in a Modal (`aria-modal=\"true\"`).\n- **Content area:** White background, `min-height: 200px`, `max-height: 480px`, overflow hidden (scrollable if content exceeds). For iframes, set `width: 100%` and auto-size height to the sanitized content.\n- **Error state:** If parsing or rendering fails, show a bordered `#FFF0F2` panel with icon ⚠, message \"Chart could not be rendered\", and the `description` text if available.\n- **Loading state:** Skeleton shimmer while sanitization completes.\n- **Accessibility:** `role=\"figure\"`, `aria-label={artifact.title}`. If `description` is present, include a visually hidden `<figcaption>` for screen readers. Expand button: `aria-label=\"Expand chart: {title}\"`.\n\n#### 5.5.4 Expand / full-screen Modal\n\n- Triggered by the `[⤢]` button or keyboard `Enter`/`Space` on it.\n- MUI `<Dialog fullWidth maxWidth=\"lg\">`, `aria-modal=\"true\"`, `aria-labelledby` pointing to the title.\n- Focus is trapped inside the modal while open. `Escape` closes it and returns focus to the expand button.\n- Modal renders the same sanitized artifact at full width.\n\n### 5.6 ThinkingIndicator\n\nShown while awaiting agent response.\n\n- Three animated dots (CSS pulse) + label \"Analyzing sensor data…\" in IBM Plex Sans 13px, colour `#4A5568`.\n- Wrap in `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Agent is analyzing sensor data\"` so screen readers announce it without being disruptive.\n- Disappears as soon as the response arrives or fails.\n\n### 5.7 InputBar\n\n- Sticky bottom, `background: #FFFFFF`, `border-top: 1px solid #D1DBE8`.\n- `<textarea>` auto-resizes (1–4 rows). Background `#F7F9FC`, border `1px solid #C9D6E3`, text `#0F1F2E`. Placeholder text `#6B7B8D` (contrast 4.6:1 — AA).\n- Visible `:focus` ring: `outline: 2px solid #005F8E`, `outline-offset: 2px` — never `outline: none`.\n- Placeholder text: `\"Ask about flood risk, AQI, traffic, power…\"`.\n- **Send button:** background `#005F8E`, icon + text label \"Send\" (visually hidden text for screen readers via `aria-label=\"Send message\"`). Disabled state: `#C9D6E3` background, `#6B7B8D` text, `aria-disabled=\"true\"`. `Enter` submits, `Shift+Enter` newline.\n- **Abort:** optional for v1. If implemented, it cancels the in-flight request and the button becomes \"Stop\" (square icon, `background: #DC2626`, `aria-label=\"Stop response\"`). Never rely on colour alone — icon + label both change.\n\n### 5.8 SuggestedPrompts\n\n---\n> **Note:** Only the last prompt reliably triggers a RiskCard — it explicitly requests a structured scorecard. The others are natural language queries that usually return plain text. Chips are for user convenience, not a trigger mechanism.\n\nClicking a chip populates and submits the textarea.\n\n---\n\n## 6. API Contract\n\n### POST `/api/chat`\n\n**Request:**\n```json\n{\n  \"session_id\": \"uuid-v4\",\n  \"message\": \"If it rains 60mm tonight, which underpasses should be barricaded based on previous years?\"\n}\n```\n\n**Response (`application/json`):**\n```json\n{\n  \"message\": \"Based on drainage capacity reports and recent rainfall patterns, Sarjapur Road has elevated flood risk tonight.\",\n  \"risk_card\": {\n    \"neighborhood\": \"SARJAPUR ROAD\",\n    \"generated_at\": \"2026-03-10T12:00:00Z\",\n    \"overall_risk_score\": 9.1\n  },\n  \"artifact\": {\n    \"type\": \"html\",\n    \"title\": \"Rainfall Trend - Sarjapur Road\",\n    \"source\": \"<div>...</div>\",\n    \"description\": \"Seven-day rainfall trend for Sarjapur Road\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n`risk_card` and `artifact` are both optional. Most responses will return `message` text only.\n\n**Error response:**\n```json\n{\n  \"error\": {\n    \"code\": \"AGENT_UNAVAILABLE\",\n    \"message\": \"The agent service is currently unavailable.\"\n  }\n}\n```\n\n**GET `/api/health`** — used by `StatusPill` to poll composite system health every 30 seconds.\n\nRecommended response shape:\n```json\n{\n  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n  \"agent\": \"ok\",\n  \"status\": \"online\"\n}\n```\n\nExample degraded response:\n```json\n{\n  \"backend\": \"ok\",\n  \"agent\": \"down\",\n  \"status\": \"degraded\"\n}\n```\n\nThe frontend behavior should be:\n- render `message` as the assistant reply\n- render `risk_card` only when the response includes it\n- render `artifact` only when the response includes it\n- show degraded state when backend is reachable but agent health is not `ok`\n\n---\n\n## 7. Risk Card JSON Schema\n\nThe agent returns this as a `risk_card` payload when it determines a structured neighborhood scorecard is the appropriate response. The frontend renders it as a RiskCard component. All top-level metric fields are optional — the agent only populates fields it has sufficient data to compute.\n\n```typescript\ninterface RiskCardPayload {\n  neighborhood: string;           // e.g. \"SARJAPUR ROAD\", \"BELLANDUR\", \"MANYATA TECH PARK\"\n  generated_at: string;           // ISO 8601 — timestamp of agent analysis\n  overall_risk_score: number;     // 0–10 composite score\n\n  // Cross-reference: DB rainfall forecast + drainage capacity docs + lake telemetry\n  flood_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Cross-reference: wind-speed telemetry + tree-density maps + historical outage logs\n  power_outage_risk?: {\n    probability: number;          // 0–1\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: rainfall threshold / traffic bottleneck historical correlation\n  traffic_delay_index?: {\n    congestion_score: number;     // 0–10\n    severity: \"CRITICAL\" | \"HIGH\" | \"MODERATE\" | \"LOW\";\n  };\n\n  // Derived from: AQI sensor data + Urban Heat Island research docs\n  health_advisory?: {\n    aqi: number;                  // raw AQI value\n    aqi_category: string;         // e.g. \"GOOD\", \"MODERATE\", \"UNHEALTHY FOR SENSITIVE GROUPS\", \"UNHEALTHY\", \"HAZARDOUS\"\n  };\n\n  // Agent's final recommendations based on full cross-analysis\n  emergency_readiness?: {\n    recommendation: string;       // e.g. \"Evacuate low-lying zones; barricade underpasses\"\n    actions?: string[];           // Specific action items, e.g. [\"Barricade Silk Board underpass\", \"Pre-position pumps at Bellandur inlet\"]\n  };\n}\n```\n\n---\n\n## 8. State Management\n\nNo external state library needed. Use React `useState` + `useRef`.\n\n```typescript\n// ChatPage state\nconst [messages, setMessages] = useState<Message[]>([]);\nconst [input, setInput] = useState('');\nconst [isSending, setIsSending] = useState(false);\nconst [agentStatus, setAgentStatus] = useState<'online' | 'connecting' | 'degraded'>('connecting');\nconst sessionId = useRef(crypto.randomUUID());\n```\n\n```typescript\ninterface ArtifactPayload {\n  type: 'html';\n  title: string;\n  source: string;              // raw HTML string from the agent\n  description?: string;        // plain-text alt for screen readers / error fallback\n}\n\ninterface Message {\n  id: string;\n  role: 'user' | 'assistant';\n  content: string;\n  riskCard?: RiskCardPayload;  // optional structured block\n  artifact?: ArtifactPayload;  // optional chart / visualisation artifact\n  timestamp: Date;\n  isError?: boolean;\n}\n```\n\nArtifact sanitization state is managed separately inside `ArtifactRenderer` — it does not live on `Message` to avoid polluting the message store with transient UI state.\n\n---\n\n## 9. File Structure\n\n```\nclient/src/\n├── App.tsx                        # Single route → ChatPage\n├── main.tsx                       # Providers (ThemeProvider, etc.)\n├── lib/\n│   ├── api.ts                     # Axios instance (already in scaffold)\n│   └── artifactSandbox.ts         # DOMPurify wrapper + iframe srcDoc helpers\n├── hooks/\n│   └── useChat.ts                 # All chat logic: send request, map response, manage session\n├── components/\n│   ├── layout/\n│   │   └── Header.tsx\n│   ├── chat/\n\n---\n    error:   { main: '#C0213A' },\n    warning: { main: '#B07D0A' },\n    success: { main: '#16863E' },\n    text: { primary: '#0F1F2E', secondary: '#4A5568', disabled: '#6B7B8D' }\n  },\n  typography: {\n    fontFamily: \"'IBM Plex Sans', sans-serif\",\n    fontSize: 14,\n    // Mono variant: sx={{ fontFamily: 'IBM Plex Mono, monospace' }}\n  },\n  components: {\n    MuiButtonBase: {\n      defaultProps: { disableRipple: false },\n      styleOverrides: {\n        root: {\n          '&:focus-visible': {\n            outline: '2px solid #005F8E',\n            outlineOffset: '2px',\n          }\n        }\n      }\n    }\n  }\n}\n```\n\n### 10.3 Accessibility Requirements\n\n- **Focus rings:** Every interactive element must show a visible `2px solid #005F8E` focus ring on keyboard focus. Never suppress `outline`.\n- **Colour independence:** Every status, severity, and state must also communicate via text label or icon — not colour alone.\n- **Touch targets:** All buttons and chips minimum `44 × 44px` tap target (WCAG 2.5.5).\n- **Motion:** Wrap all non-essential animations in `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` — disable or reduce them.\n- **Semantic HTML:** Use `<main>`, `<header>`, `<section aria-label>` landmark roles. Message list: `role=\"log\" aria-live=\"polite\" aria-label=\"Conversation\"`. Streaming indicator: `role=\"status\" aria-live=\"polite\"`.\n- **Minimum font size:** 13px for any visible text. Secondary/timestamp labels may be 11px only if colour contrast ≥ 7:1.\n- **Link underlines:** Inline links in message text must be underlined (not colour-only).\n- **Custom scrollbar:** Thin, `#C9D6E3` track, `#005F8E` thumb. Ensure scrollbar thumb contrast ≥ 3:1 against track.\n\n---\n\n## 11. Environment Variables\n\nAdd to the scaffold's existing env var pattern:\n\n| Variable | Default | Description |\n|----------|---------|-------------|\n| `AGENT_SERVER_URL` | `http://agents:8020` | Internal Agent Server URL (Docker Compose service name). Used by the backend to call the internal agent service. |\n| `VITE_API_BASE_URL` | *(proxied via Vite → :8010)* | Frontend API base (already handled by Vite proxy in scaffold) |\n\n---\n\n## 12. Backend Route to Add\n\nAdd one route to `server/app/api/routes/`:\n\n```\nserver/app/api/routes/chat.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- Accept `POST /api/chat` with `{ session_id, message }`.\n- Call the Agent Server via `agent_proxy.get_chat_response()`.\n- Return a single JSON response with `message`, optional `risk_card`, and optional `artifact`.\n- Accept `GET /api/health` and return composite backend + agent health for the frontend status pill.\n- Accept `DELETE /api/chat/{session_id}` to clear agent session state if session reset is implemented.\n\nAdd one service:\n\n```\nserver/app/services/agent_proxy.py\n```\n\nResponsibilities:\n- `async def get_chat_response(session_id, message) -> ChatResponse`\n- Call the internal `agents/` service over HTTP at `AGENT_SERVER_URL`.\n- Normalize agent failures into backend-owned error responses.\n- Handle timeout (30s) and connection errors cleanly.\n\nAdd to `docker-compose.yml`:\n\n```yaml\nagents:\n  build: ./agents         # your Agent Server directory\n  ports:\n    - \"8020:8020\"         # internal service port\n  depends_on:\n    - db\n"
  }
}
```
